./README.md:3:⚡ Building LLM-powered applications in Ruby ⚡
./README.md:5:For deep Rails integration see: [langchainrb_rails](https://github.com/patterns-ai-core/langchainrb_rails) gem.
./README.md:24:- [Unified Interface for LLMs](#unified-interface-for-llms)
./README.md:54:# Unified Interface for LLMs
./README.md:56:The `Langchain::LLM` module provides a unified interface for interacting with various Large Language Model (LLM) providers. This abstraction allows you to easily switch between different LLM backends without changing your application code.
./README.md:58:## Supported LLM Providers
./README.md:74:All LLM classes inherit from `Langchain::LLM::Base` and provide a consistent interface for common operations:
./README.md:82:Most LLM classes can be initialized with an API key and optional default options:
./README.md:85:llm = Langchain::LLM::OpenAI.new(
./README.md:136:response = llm.chat(messages: messages)
./README.md:140:#### Accepted parameters for `chat()`
./README.md:153:- `tools`: (Optional) A list of tools the model may call.
./README.md:154:- `tool_choice`: (Optional) Controls how the model calls functions.
./README.md:156:## Switching LLM Providers
./README.md:158:Thanks to the unified interface, you can easily switch between different LLM providers by changing the class you instantiate:
./README.md:162:anthropic_llm = Langchain::LLM::Anthropic.new(api_key: ENV["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"])
./README.md:165:gemini_llm = Langchain::LLM::GoogleGemini.new(api_key: ENV["GOOGLE_GEMINI_API_KEY"])
./README.md:168:openai_llm = Langchain::LLM::OpenAI.new(api_key: ENV["OPENAI_API_KEY"])
./README.md:173:Each LLM method returns a response object that provides a consistent interface for accessing the results:
./README.md:178:- `tool_calls`: Returns tool calls made by the LLM
./README.md:184:> While the core interface is consistent across providers, some LLMs may offer additional features or parameters. Consult the documentation for each LLM class to learn about provider-specific capabilities and options.
./README.md:274:Parse LLM text responses into structured output, such as JSON.
./README.md:278:You can use the `StructuredOutputParser` to generate a prompt that instructs the LLM to provide a JSON response adhering to a specific JSON schema:
./README.md:328:llm = Langchain::LLM::OpenAI.new(api_key: ENV["OPENAI_API_KEY"])
./README.md:329:llm_response = llm.chat(messages: [{role: "user", content: prompt_text}]).completion
./README.md:344:If the parser fails to parse the LLM response, you can use the `OutputFixingParser`. It sends an error message, prior output, and the original prompt text to the LLM, asking for a "fixed" response:
./README.md:373:RAG is a methodology that assists LLMs generate accurate and up-to-date information.
./README.md:377:3. LLM receives the prompt above to generate a text completion.
./README.md:403:Choose and instantiate the LLM provider you'll be using to generate embeddings
./README.md:405:llm = Langchain::LLM::OpenAI.new(api_key: ENV["OPENAI_API_KEY"])
./README.md:480:`Langchain::Assistant` is a powerful and flexible class that combines Large Language Models (LLMs), tools, and conversation management to create intelligent, interactive assistants. It's designed to handle complex conversations, execute tools, and provide coherent responses based on the context of the interaction.
./README.md:483:* Supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Google Gemini, Anthropic, Mistral AI and open-source models via Ollama)
./README.md:484:* Integrates with various tools to extend functionality
./README.md:486:* Handles automatic and manual tool execution
./README.md:487:* Supports different message formats for various LLM providers
./README.md:491:llm = Langchain::LLM::OpenAI.new(api_key: ENV["OPENAI_API_KEY"])
./README.md:495:  tools: [Langchain::Tool::NewsRetriever.new(api_key: ENV["NEWS_API_KEY"])]
./README.md:510:# Run the assistant with automatic tool execution
./README.md:511:assistant.run(auto_tool_execution: true)
./README.md:517:  tools: [Langchain::Tool::NewsRetriever.new(api_key: ENV["NEWS_API_KEY"])]
./README.md:523:assistant.run(auto_tool_execution: true)
./README.md:526:Note that streaming is not currently supported for all LLMs.
./README.md:529:* `llm`: The LLM instance to use (required)
./README.md:530:* `tools`: An array of tool instances (optional)
./README.md:532:* `tool_choice`: Specifies how tools should be selected. Default: "auto". A specific tool function name can be passed. This will force the Assistant to **always** use this function.
./README.md:533:* `parallel_tool_calls`: Whether to make multiple parallel tool calls. Default: true
./README.md:538:* `tool_execution_callback`: A callback function (proc, lambda) that is called right before a tool is executed (optional)
./README.md:540:assistant.tool_execution_callback = -> (tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments) { puts "Executing tool_call_id: #{tool_call_id}, tool_name: #{tool_name}, method_name: #{method_name}, tool_arguments: #{tool_arguments}" }
./README.md:547:* `submit_tool_output`: Manually submit output to a tool call
./README.md:550:### Built-in Tools 🛠️
./README.md:551:* `Langchain::Tool::Calculator`: Useful for evaluating math expressions. Requires `gem "eqn"`.
./README.md:552:* `Langchain::Tool::Database`: Connect your SQL database. Requires `gem "sequel"`.
./README.md:553:* `Langchain::Tool::FileSystem`: Interact with the file system (read & write).
./README.md:554:* `Langchain::Tool::GoogleSearch`: Wrapper around SerpApi's Google Search API. Requires `gem "google_search_results"`.
./README.md:555:* `Langchain::Tool::NewsRetriever`: A wrapper around [NewsApi.org](https://newsapi.org) to fetch news articles.
./README.md:556:* `Langchain::Tool::RubyCodeInterpreter`: Useful for evaluating generated Ruby code. Requires `gem "safe_ruby"` (In need of a better solution).
./README.md:557:* `Langchain::Tool::Tavily`: A wrapper around [Tavily AI](https://tavily.com).
./README.md:558:* `Langchain::Tool::Vectorsearch`: A wrapper for vector search classes.
./README.md:559:* `Langchain::Tool::Weather`: Calls [Open Weather API](https://home.openweathermap.org) to retrieve the current weather.
./README.md:560:* `Langchain::Tool::Wikipedia`: Calls Wikipedia API. Requires `gem "wikipedia-client"`.
./README.md:562:### Creating custom Tools
./README.md:563:The Langchain::Assistant can be easily extended with custom tools by creating classes that `extend Langchain::ToolDefinition` module and implement required methods.
./README.md:565:class MovieInfoTool

./CHANGELOG.md:114:- Improve Langchain::Tool::Weather tool

I found the main shapes: `lib/langchain/assistant*` owns tool-calling orchestration, `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters*` bridges providers, and `lib/langchain/llm*` holds raw provider clients and response wrappers. I’m drilling into those files now for exact contracts and line numbers.

     7	      class Adapter
     8	        def self.build(llm)
     9	          if llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::Anthropic)
    10	            LLM::Adapters::Anthropic.new
    11	          elsif llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::AwsBedrock) && llm.defaults[:chat_model].include?("anthropic")
    12	            LLM::Adapters::AwsBedrockAnthropic.new
    13	          elsif llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::GoogleGemini) || llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::GoogleVertexAI)
    14	            LLM::Adapters::GoogleGemini.new
    15	          elsif llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::MistralAI)
    16	            LLM::Adapters::MistralAI.new
    17	          elsif llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::Ollama)

    20	            LLM::Adapters::OpenAI.new
    21	          else
    22	            raise ArgumentError, "Unsupported LLM type: #{llm.class}"
    23	          end
    24	        end
    25	      end
    26	    end
    27	  end

     3	module Langchain
     4	  # Assistants are Agent-like objects that leverage helpful instructions, LLMs, tools and knowledge to respond to user queries.
     5	  # Assistants can be configured with an LLM of your choice, any vector search database and easily extended with additional tools.
     6	  #
     7	  # Usage:
     8	  #     llm = Langchain::LLM::GoogleGemini.new(api_key: ENV["GOOGLE_GEMINI_API_KEY"])
     9	  #     assistant = Langchain::Assistant.new(
    10	  #       llm: llm,
    11	  #       instructions: "You're a News Reporter AI",
    12	  #       tools: [Langchain::Tool::NewsRetriever.new(api_key: ENV["NEWS_API_KEY"])]
    13	  #     )
    14	  class Assistant
    15	    attr_reader :llm,
    16	      :instructions,
    17	      :state,
    18	      :llm_adapter,
    19	      :messages,
    20	      :tool_choice,
    21	      :total_prompt_tokens,
    22	      :total_completion_tokens,
    23	      :total_tokens
    24	
    25	    attr_accessor :tools,
    26	      :add_message_callback,
    27	      :tool_execution_callback,
    28	      :parallel_tool_calls
    29	
    30	    # Create a new assistant
    31	    #
    32	    # @param llm [Langchain::LLM::Base] LLM instance that the assistant will use
    33	    # @param tools [Array<Langchain::Tool::Base>] Tools that the assistant has access to
    34	    # @param instructions [String] The system instructions
    35	    # @param tool_choice [String] Specify how tools should be selected. Options: "auto", "any", "none", or <specific function name>
    36	    # @param parallel_tool_calls [Boolean] Whether or not to run tools in parallel
    37	    # @param messages [Array<Langchain::Assistant::Messages::Base>] The messages
    38	    # @param add_message_callback [Proc] A callback function (Proc or lambda) that is called when any message is added to the conversation
    39	    # @param tool_execution_callback [Proc] A callback function (Proc or lambda) that is called right before a tool function is executed
    40	    def initialize(
    41	      llm:,
    42	      tools: [],
    43	      instructions: nil,
    44	      tool_choice: "auto",
    45	      parallel_tool_calls: true,
    46	      messages: [],
    47	      # Callbacks
    48	      add_message_callback: nil,
    49	      tool_execution_callback: nil,
    50	      &block
    51	    )
    52	      unless tools.is_a?(Array) && tools.all? { |tool| tool.class.singleton_class.include?(Langchain::ToolDefinition) }
    53	        raise ArgumentError, "Tools must be an array of objects extending Langchain::ToolDefinition"
    54	      end
    55	
    56	      @llm = llm
    57	      @llm_adapter = LLM::Adapter.build(llm)
    58	
    59	      @add_message_callback = add_message_callback if validate_callback!("add_message_callback", add_message_callback)
    60	      @tool_execution_callback = tool_execution_callback if validate_callback!("tool_execution_callback", tool_execution_callback)
    61	
    62	      self.messages = messages
    63	      @tools = tools
    64	      @parallel_tool_calls = parallel_tool_calls
    65	      self.tool_choice = tool_choice
    66	      self.instructions = instructions
    67	      @block = block
    68	      @state = :ready
    69	
    70	      @total_prompt_tokens = 0
    71	      @total_completion_tokens = 0
    72	      @total_tokens = 0
    73	    end
    74	
    75	    # Add a user message to the messages array
    76	    #
    77	    # @param role [String] The role attribute of the message. Default: "user"
    78	    # @param content [String] The content of the message
    79	    # @param image_url [String] The URL of the image to include in the message
    80	    # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls to include in the message
    81	    # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call to include in the message
    82	    # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
    83	    def add_message(role: "user", content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
    84	      message = build_message(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
    85	
    86	      # Call the callback with the message
    87	      add_message_callback.call(message) if add_message_callback # rubocop:disable Style/SafeNavigation
    88	
    89	      # Prepend the message to the messages array
    90	      messages << message
    91	
    92	      @state = :ready
    93	
    94	      messages
    95	    end

    97	    # Convert messages to an LLM APIs-compatible array of hashes

    99	    # @return [Array<Hash>] Messages as an OpenAI API-compatible array of hashes
   100	    def array_of_message_hashes
   101	      messages

   103	        .compact

   112	    #

   114	    # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages

   116	      raise ArgumentError, "messages array must only contain Langchain::Message instance(s)" unless messages.is_a?(Array) && messages.all? { |m| m.is_a?(Messages::Base) }

   123	    # @param messages [Array<Hash>] The messages to add
   124	    # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
   125	    def add_messages(messages:)
   126	      messages.each do |message_hash|

   131	    # Run the assistant
   132	    #
   133	    # @param auto_tool_execution [Boolean] Whether or not to automatically run tools
   134	    # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
   135	    def run(auto_tool_execution: false)
   136	      if messages.empty?
   137	        Langchain.logger.warn("#{self.class} - No messages to process")
   138	        @state = :completed
   139	        return

   141	
   142	      @state = :in_progress
   143	      @state = handle_state until run_finished?(auto_tool_execution)
   144	
   145	      messages
   146	    end

   155	    # Add a user message and run the assistant

   215	    # Replace old system message with new one
   216	    #
   217	    # @param content [String] New system message content
   218	    # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
   219	    def replace_system_message!(content:)
   220	      messages.delete_if(&:system?)

   223	      message = build_message(role: "system", content: content)
   224	      messages.unshift(message)

   228	    def validate_tool_choice!(tool_choice)
   229	      allowed_tool_choices = llm_adapter.allowed_tool_choices.concat(available_tool_names)
   230	      unless allowed_tool_choices.include?(tool_choice)

   235	    # Check if the run is finished
   236	    #
   237	    # @param auto_tool_execution [Boolean] Whether or not to automatically run tools
   238	    # @return [Boolean] Whether the run is finished
   239	    def run_finished?(auto_tool_execution)
   240	      finished_states = [:completed, :failed]
   241	
   242	      requires_manual_action = (@state == :requires_action) && !auto_tool_execution
   243	      finished_states.include?(@state) || requires_manual_action

   246	    # Handle the current state and transition to the next state
   247	    #
   248	    # @return [Symbol] The next state
   249	    def handle_state
   250	      case @state
   251	      when :in_progress
   252	        process_latest_message
   253	      when :requires_action
   254	        execute_tools
   255	      end
   256	    end
   257	
   258	    # Process the latest message
   259	    #
   260	    # @return [Symbol] The next state
   261	    def process_latest_message
   262	      last_message = messages.last
   263	
   264	      case last_message.standard_role
   265	      when :system
   266	        handle_system_message
   267	      when :llm
   268	        handle_llm_message
   269	      when :user, :tool
   270	        handle_user_or_tool_message
   271	      else
   272	        handle_unexpected_message
   273	      end
   274	    end

   292	    #
   293	    # @return [Symbol] The failed state

   297	    end

   299	    # Handle user or tool message scenario by processing the LLM response

   304	
   305	      add_message(role: response.role, content: response.chat_completion, tool_calls: response.tool_calls)
   306	      record_used_tokens(response.prompt_tokens, response.completion_tokens, response.total_tokens)

   311	    def set_state_for(response:)
   312	      if response.tool_calls.any?
   313	        :in_progress
   314	      elsif response.chat_completion
   315	        :completed
   316	      elsif response.completion # Currently only used by Ollama

   339	      Langchain.logger.debug("#{self.class} - Sending a call to #{llm.class}")
   340	
   341	      params = @llm_adapter.build_chat_params(
   342	        instructions: @instructions,
   343	        messages: array_of_message_hashes,
   344	        tools: @tools,
   345	        tool_choice: tool_choice,

   353	    # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls to run
   354	    def run_tools(tool_calls)
   355	      # Iterate over each function invocation and submit tool output
   356	      tool_calls.each do |tool_call|
   357	        run_tool(tool_call)
   358	      end
   359	    end
   360	
   361	    # Run the tool call
   362	    #
   363	    # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call to run
   364	    # @return [Object] The result of the tool call
   365	    def run_tool(tool_call)
   366	      tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments = @llm_adapter.extract_tool_call_args(tool_call: tool_call)
   367	
   368	      tool_instance = tools.find do |t|
   369	        t.class.tool_name == tool_name
   370	      end or raise ArgumentError, "Tool: #{tool_name} not found in assistant.tools"
   371	
   372	      # Call the callback if set
   373	      tool_execution_callback.call(tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments) if tool_execution_callback # rubocop:disable Style/SafeNavigation
   374	
   375	      output = tool_instance.public_send(method_name, **tool_arguments)
   376	
   377	      # Handle both ToolResponse and legacy return values
   378	      if output.is_a?(ToolResponse)
   379	        add_message(role: @llm_adapter.tool_role, content: output.content, image_url: output.image_url, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
   380	      else
   381	        submit_tool_output(tool_call_id: tool_call_id, output: output)
   382	      end
   383	    end
   384	
   385	    # Build a message
   386	    #
   387	    # @param role [String] The role of the message
   388	    # @param content [String] The content of the message
   389	    # @param image_url [String] The URL of the image to include in the message
   390	    # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls to include in the message
   391	    # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call to include in the message
   392	    # @return [Langchain::Message] The Message object
   393	    def build_message(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
   394	      @llm_adapter.build_message(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
   395	    end
   396	
   397	    # Increment the tokens count based on the last interaction with the LLM
   398	    #
   399	    # @param prompt_tokens [Integer] The number of used prmopt tokens
   400	    # @param completion_tokens [Integer] The number of used completion tokens
   401	    # @param total_tokens [Integer] The total number of used tokens
   402	    # @return [Integer] The current total tokens count
   403	    def record_used_tokens(prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens_from_operation)
   404	      @total_prompt_tokens += prompt_tokens if prompt_tokens
   405	      @total_completion_tokens += completion_tokens if completion_tokens
   406	      @total_tokens += total_tokens_from_operation if total_tokens_from_operation
   407	    end
   408	
   409	    def available_tool_names
   410	      llm_adapter.available_tool_names(tools)
   411	    end
   412	
   413	    def validate_callback!(attr_name, callback)
   414	      if !callback.nil? && !callback.respond_to?(:call)
   415	        raise ArgumentError, "#{attr_name} must be a callable object, like Proc or lambda"
   416	      end
   417	
   418	      true
   419	    end
   420	  end
   421	end

FILE lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/anthropic.rb
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Langchain
     4	  class Assistant
     5	    module LLM
     6	      module Adapters
     7	        class Anthropic < Base
     8	          # Build the chat parameters for the Anthropic API
     9	          #
    10	          # @param messages [Array<Hash>] The messages
    11	          # @param instructions [String] The system instructions
    12	          # @param tools [Array<Hash>] The tools to use
    13	          # @param tool_choice [String] The tool choice
    14	          # @param parallel_tool_calls [Boolean] Whether to make parallel tool calls
    15	          # @return [Hash] The chat parameters
    16	          def build_chat_params(
    17	            messages:,
    18	            instructions:,
    19	            tools:,
    20	            tool_choice:,
    21	            parallel_tool_calls:
    22	          )
    23	            params = {messages: messages}
    24	            if tools.any?
    25	              params[:tools] = build_tools(tools)
    26	              params[:tool_choice] = build_tool_choice(tool_choice, parallel_tool_calls)
    27	            end
    28	            params[:system] = instructions if instructions
    29	            params
    30	          end
    31	
    32	          # Build an Anthropic message
    33	          #
    34	          # @param role [String] The role of the message
    35	          # @param content [String] The content of the message
    36	          # @param image_url [String] The image URL
    37	          # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls
    38	          # @param tool_call_id [String] The tool call ID
    39	          # @return [Messages::AnthropicMessage] The Anthropic message
    40	          def build_message(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
    41	            Messages::AnthropicMessage.new(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
    42	          end
    43	
    44	          # Extract the tool call information from the Anthropic tool call hash
    45	          #
    46	          # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call hash, format: {"type"=>"tool_use", "id"=>"toolu_01TjusbFApEbwKPRWTRwzadR", "name"=>"news_retriever__get_top_headlines", "input"=>{"country"=>"us", "page_size"=>10}}], "stop_reason"=>"tool_use"}
    47	          # @return [Array] The tool call information
    48	          def extract_tool_call_args(tool_call:)
    49	            tool_call_id = tool_call.dig(:id)
    50	            function_name = tool_call.dig(:name)
    51	            tool_name, method_name = function_name.split("__")
    52	            tool_arguments = tool_call.dig(:input).transform_keys(&:to_sym)
    53	            [tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments]
    54	          end
    55	
    56	          # Build the tools for the Anthropic API
    57	          def build_tools(tools)
    58	            tools.map { |tool| tool.class.function_schemas.to_anthropic_format }.flatten
    59	          end
    60	
    61	          # Get the allowed assistant.tool_choice values for Anthropic
    62	          def allowed_tool_choices
    63	            ["auto", "any"]
    64	          end
    65	
    66	          # Get the available tool function names for Anthropic
    67	          #
    68	          # @param tools [Array<Hash>] The tools
    69	          # @return [Array<String>] The tool function names
    70	          def available_tool_names(tools)
    71	            build_tools(tools).map { |tool| tool.dig(:name) }
    72	          end
    73	
    74	          def tool_role
    75	            Messages::AnthropicMessage::TOOL_ROLE
    76	          end
    77	
    78	          def support_system_message?
    79	            Messages::AnthropicMessage::ROLES.include?("system")
    80	          end
    81	
    82	          private
    83	
    84	          def build_tool_choice(choice, parallel_tool_calls)
    85	            tool_choice_object = {disable_parallel_tool_use: !parallel_tool_calls}
    86	
    87	            case choice
    88	            when "auto"
    89	              tool_choice_object[:type] = "auto"
    90	            when "any"
    91	              tool_choice_object[:type] = "any"
    92	            else
    93	              tool_choice_object[:type] = "tool"
    94	              tool_choice_object[:name] = choice
    95	            end
    96	
    97	            tool_choice_object
    98	          end
    99	        end
   100	      end
   101	    end
   102	  end
   103	end

     3	module Langchain
     4	  class Assistant
     5	    module LLM

     7	        class GoogleGemini < Base
     8	          # Build the chat parameters for the Google Gemini LLM
     9	          #
    10	          # @param messages [Array] The messages

    16	          def build_chat_params(

    22	          )

    24

    26	            if tools.any?
    27	              params[:tools] = build_tools(tools)
    28	              params[:system] = instructions if instructions
    29	              params[:tool_choice] = build_tool_config(tool_choice)
    30	            end
    31	            params
    32	          end
    33	
    34	          # Build a Google Gemini message
    35	          #
    36	          # @param role [String] The role of the message
    37	          # @param content [String] The content of the message
    38	          # @param image_url [String] The image URL
    39	          # @param tool_calls [Array] The tool calls
    40	          # @param tool_call_id [String] The tool call ID
    41	          # @return [Messages::GoogleGeminiMessage] The Google Gemini message
    42	          def build_message(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
    43	            Langchain.logger.warn "Image URL is not supported by Google Gemini" if image_url
    44	
    45	            Messages::GoogleGeminiMessage.new(role: role, content: content, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
    46	          end

    49	          #
    50	          # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call hash, format: {"functionCall"=>{"name"=>"weather__execute", "args"=>{"input"=>"NYC"}}}
    51	          # @return [Array] The tool call information
    52	          def extract_tool_call_args(tool_call:)
    53	            tool_call_id = tool_call.dig("functionCall", "name")
    54	            function_name = tool_call.dig("functionCall", "name")
    55	            tool_name, method_name = function_name.split("__")
    56	            tool_arguments = tool_call.dig("functionCall", "args").transform_keys(&:to_sym)
    57	            [tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments]

    92	            when "none"
    93	              {function_calling_config: {mode: "none"}}

    19	            tools:,
    20	            tool_choice:,
    21	            parallel_tool_calls:
    22	          )
    23	            params = {messages: messages}
    24	            if tools.any?
    25	              params[:tools] = build_tools(tools)
    26	              params[:tool_choice] = build_tool_choice(tool_choice)

    43	            Messages::OpenAIMessage.new(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
    44	          end

    47	          #
    48	          # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call hash
    49	          # @return [Array] The tool call information
    50	          def extract_tool_call_args(tool_call:)
    51	            tool_call_id = tool_call.dig("id")
    52	
    53	            function_name = tool_call.dig("function", "name")
    54	            tool_name, method_name = function_name.split("__")
    55	
    56	            tool_arguments = tool_call.dig("function", "arguments")
    57	            tool_arguments = if tool_arguments.is_a?(Hash)
    58	              Langchain::Utils::HashTransformer.symbolize_keys(tool_arguments)
    59	            else

    61	            end
    62

    68	            tools.map { |tool| tool.class.function_schemas.to_openai_format }.flatten

    92	            case choice
    93	            when "auto"

    95	            else

    97	            end
    98	          end
    99	        end

   101	    end
   102	  end
   103	end

The adapter contract is explicit but not fully captured by `Base`: real adapters also implement `allowed_tool_choices`, `available_tool_names`, and often `build_tools`. I’m now mapping message and response wrappers, then the raw client classes that return those responses.

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Langchain::LLM
     4	  class UnifiedParameters
     5	    include Enumerable
     6	
     7	    attr_reader :schema, :aliases, :parameters, :ignored, :remapped
     8	
     9	    class Null < self
    10	      def initialize(parameters: {})
    11	        super(schema: {}, parameters: parameters)
    12	      end
    13	    end
    14	
    15	    def initialize(schema:, parameters: {})
    16	      @schema = schema || {}
    17	      @aliases = {}
    18	      @remapped = {}
    19	      @ignored = Set.new
    20	      @schema.each do |name, param|
    21	        @aliases[name] = Set.new(Array(param[:aliases])) if param[:aliases]
    22	      end
    23	      @parameters = to_params(parameters.to_h) if !parameters.to_h.empty?
    24	    end
    25	
    26	    def to_params(params = {})
    27	      # if params are provided, reset any previously initialized
    28	      @parameters = params if !params.empty?
    29	      @parameters = (@parameters || {}).merge!(params).slice(*schema.keys)
    30	      @aliases.each do |field, aliased_keys|
    31	        # favor existing keys in case of conflicts,
    32	        # and check for multiples
    33	        aliased_keys.each do |alias_key|
    34	          @parameters[field] ||= params[alias_key] if value_present?(params[alias_key])
    35	        end
    36	      end
    37	      @schema.each do |field, param_options|
    38	        param_options ||= {}
    39	        default = param_options[:default]
    40	        @parameters[field] ||= default if value_present?(default)
    41	      end
    42	      @remapped.each do |field, renamed_field|
    43	        @parameters[renamed_field] = @parameters[field] if value_present?(@parameters[field])
    44	      end
    45	      @parameters = @parameters.except(*@ignored + @remapped.keys)
    46	    end
    47	
    48	    def remap(field_map)
    49	      @remapped ||= {}
    50	      @remapped.merge!(field_map)
    51	      field_map.each do |field, renamed_field|
    52	        @schema[renamed_field] = @schema[field]
    53	      end
    54	    end
    55	
    56	    def update(schema = {})
    57	      @schema.merge!(schema)
    58	      schema.each do |name, param|
    59	        if param[:aliases]
    60	          @aliases[name] ||= Set.new
    61	          @aliases[name] << param[:aliases]
    62	        end
    63	      end
    64	      self
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    def ignore(*field_names)
    68	      @ignored.merge(field_names)
    69	    end
    70	
    71	    def alias_field(field_name, as:)
    72	      @aliases[field_name] ||= Set.new
    73	      @aliases[field_name] << as
    74	    end
    75	
    76	    def to_h
    77	      @parameters.to_h
    78	    end
    79	
    80	    def each(&block)
    81	      to_params.each(&block)
    82	    end
    83	
    84	    def <=>(other)
    85	      to_params.<=>(other.to_params)
    86	    end
    87	
    88	    def [](key)
    89	      to_params[key]
    90	    end
    91	
    92	    private
    93	
    94	    def value_present?(value)
    95	      !value.nil? && (!value.is_a?(Enumerable) || !value.empty?)
    96	    end
    97	  end
    98	end

     8	    # future, we may consider ActiveModel-style validations and further typed
     9	    # options here.
    10	    SCHEMA = {
    11	      # Either "messages" or "prompt" is required
    12	      messages: {},
    13	      model: {},
    14	      prompt: {},
    15	
    16	      # System instructions. Used by Cohere, Anthropic and Google Gemini.

    21

    23	      stream: {}, # Enable streaming
    24	
    25	      max_tokens: {}, # Range: [1, context_length)
    26	      temperature: {}, # Range: [0, 2]
    27	      top_p: {}, # Range: (0, 1]
    28	      top_k: {}, # Range: [1, Infinity) Not available for OpenAI models
    29	      frequency_penalty: {}, # Range: [-2, 2]
    30	      presence_penalty: {}, # Range: [-2, 2]
    31	      repetition_penalty: {}, # Range: (0, 2]
    32	      seed: {}, # OpenAI only
    33	      store: {}, # store messages with OpenAI
    34	
    35	      # Function-calling
    36	      tools: {default: []},
    37	      tool_choice: {},
    38	      parallel_tool_calls: {},
    39	
    40	      # Additional optional parameters
    41	      logit_bias: {},
    42	
    43	      # Additional llm options. Ollama only.
    44	      options: {}
    45	    }
    46	
    47	    def initialize(parameters: {})
    48	      super(

    50	          schema: SCHEMA.dup,
    51	          parameters: parameters
    52	        )
    53	      )
    54	    end
    55	  end

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Langchain
     4	  class Assistant
     5	    module Messages
     6	      class AnthropicMessage < Base
     7	        ROLES = [
     8	          "assistant",
     9	          "user",
    10	          "tool_result"
    11	        ].freeze
    12	
    13	        TOOL_ROLE = "tool_result"
    14	
    15	        # Initialize a new Anthropic message
    16	        #
    17	        # @param role [String] The role of the message
    18	        # @param content [String] The content of the message
    19	        # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls made in the message
    20	        # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call
    21	        def initialize(
    22	          role:,
    23	          content: nil,
    24	          image_url: nil,
    25	          tool_calls: [],
    26	          tool_call_id: nil
    27	        )
    28	          raise ArgumentError, "Role must be one of #{ROLES.join(", ")}" unless ROLES.include?(role)
    29	          raise ArgumentError, "Tool calls must be an array of hashes" unless tool_calls.is_a?(Array) && tool_calls.all? { |tool_call| tool_call.is_a?(Hash) }
    30	
    31	          @role = role
    32	          # Some Tools return content as a JSON hence `.to_s`
    33	          @content = content.to_s
    34	          @image_url = image_url
    35	          @tool_calls = tool_calls
    36	          @tool_call_id = tool_call_id
    37	        end
    38	
    39	        # Convert the message to an Anthropic API-compatible hash
    40	        #
    41	        # @return [Hash] The message as an Anthropic API-compatible hash
    42	        def to_hash
    43	          if assistant?
    44	            assistant_hash
    45	          elsif tool?
    46	            tool_hash
    47	          elsif user?
    48	            user_hash
    49	          end
    50	        end
    51	
    52	        # Convert the message to an Anthropic API-compatible hash
    53	        #
    54	        # @return [Hash] The message as an Anthropic API-compatible hash, with the role as "assistant"
    55	        def assistant_hash
    56	          content_array = []
    57	          if content && !content.empty?
    58	            content_array << {type: "text", text: content}
    59	          end
    60	
    61	          {
    62	            role: "assistant",
    63	            content: content_array.concat(tool_calls)
    64	          }
    65	        end
    66	
    67	        # Convert the message to an Anthropic API-compatible hash
    68	        #
    69	        # @return [Hash] The message as an Anthropic API-compatible hash, with the role as "user"
    70	        def tool_hash
    71	          {
    72	            role: "user",
    73	            # TODO: Tool can also return images
    74	            # https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/tool-use#handling-tool-use-and-tool-result-content-blocks
    75	            content: [
    76	              {
    77	                type: "tool_result",
    78	                tool_use_id: tool_call_id,
    79	                content: content
    80	              }
    81	            ]
    82	          }
    83	        end
    84	
    85	        # Convert the message to an Anthropic API-compatible hash
    86	        #
    87	        # @return [Hash] The message as an Anthropic API-compatible hash, with the role as "user"
    88	        def user_hash
    89	          {
    90	            role: "user",
    91	            content: build_content_array
    92	          }
    93	        end
    94	
    95	        # Builds the content value for the message hash
    96	        # @return [Array<Hash>] An array of content hashes
    97	        def build_content_array
    98	          content_details = []
    99	
   100	          if content && !content.empty?
   101	            content_details << {
   102	              type: "text",
   103	              text: content
   104	            }
   105	          end
   106	
   107	          if image
   108	            content_details << {
   109	              type: "image",
   110	              source: {
   111	                type: "base64",
   112	                data: image.base64,
   113	                media_type: image.mime_type
   114	              }
   115	            }
   116	          end
   117	
   118	          content_details
   119	        end
   120	
   121	        # Check if the message is a tool call
   122	        #
   123	        # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message is a tool call
   124	        def tool?
   125	          role == "tool_result"
   126	        end
   127	
   128	        # Anthropic does not implement system prompts
   129	        def system?
   130	          false
   131	        end
   132	
   133	        # Check if the message came from an LLM
   134	        #
   135	        # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
   136	        def assistant?
   137	          role == "assistant"
   138	        end
   139	
   140	        # Check if the message came from an LLM
   141	        #
   142	        # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
   143	        def llm?
   144	          assistant?
   145	        end
   146	      end
   147	    end
   148	  end
   149	end

     3	module Langchain
     4	  class Assistant
     5	    module Messages
     6	      class Base

     8	          :content,
     9	          :image_url,
    10	          :tool_calls,

    12	
    13	        # Check if the message came from a user

    19	
    20	        # Check if the message came from an LLM
    21	        #
    22	        # @raise NotImplementedError if the subclass does not implement this method
    23	        def llm?
    24	          raise NotImplementedError, "Class #{self.class.name} must implement the method 'llm?'"
    25	        end
    26	
    27	        # Check if the message is a tool call
    28	        #
    29	        # @raise NotImplementedError if the subclass does not implement this method
    30	        def tool?
    31	          raise NotImplementedError, "Class #{self.class.name} must implement the method 'tool?'"
    32	        end
    33	
    34	        # Check if the message is a system prompt
    35	        #
    36	        # @raise NotImplementedError if the subclass does not implement this method
    37	        def system?
    38	          raise NotImplementedError, "Class #{self.class.name} must implement the method 'system?'"
    39	        end
    40	
    41	        # Returns the standardized role symbol based on the specific role methods
    42	        #
    43	        # @return [Symbol] the standardized role symbol (:system, :llm, :tool, :user, or :unknown)
    44	        def standard_role
    45	          return :user if user?

    47	          return :tool if tool?
    48	          return :system if system?

    51	          :unknown

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Langchain
     4	  class Assistant
     5	    module Messages
     6	      class OllamaMessage < Base
     7	        # OpenAI uses the following roles:
     8	        ROLES = [
     9	          "system",
    10	          "assistant",
    11	          "user",
    12	          "tool"
    13	        ].freeze
    14	
    15	        TOOL_ROLE = "tool"
    16	
    17	        # Initialize a new OpenAI message
    18	        #
    19	        # @param role [String] The role of the message
    20	        # @param content [String] The content of the message
    21	        # @param image_url [String] The URL of the image to include in the message
    22	        # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls made in the message
    23	        # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call
    24	        def initialize(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
    25	          raise ArgumentError, "Role must be one of #{ROLES.join(", ")}" unless ROLES.include?(role)
    26	          raise ArgumentError, "Tool calls must be an array of hashes" unless tool_calls.is_a?(Array) && tool_calls.all? { |tool_call| tool_call.is_a?(Hash) }
    27	
    28	          # Ensure compatibility with `URI::RFC2396_PARSER` (Ruby 3.4+) and `URI::DEFAULT_PARSER` (<= 3.3).
    29	          uri_parser = defined?(URI::RFC2396_PARSER) ? URI::RFC2396_PARSER : URI::DEFAULT_PARSER
    30	          raise ArgumentError, "image_url must be a valid url" if image_url && !uri_parser.make_regexp.match?(image_url)
    31	
    32	          @role = role
    33	          # Some Tools return content as a JSON hence `.to_s`
    34	          @content = content.to_s
    35	          @image_url = image_url
    36	          @tool_calls = tool_calls
    37	          @tool_call_id = tool_call_id
    38	        end
    39	
    40	        # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
    41	        #
    42	        # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash
    43	        def to_hash
    44	          {}.tap do |h|
    45	            h[:role] = role
    46	            h[:content] = content if content # Content is nil for tool calls
    47	            h[:images] = [image.base64] if image
    48	            h[:tool_calls] = tool_calls if tool_calls.any?
    49	            h[:tool_call_id] = tool_call_id if tool_call_id
    50	          end
    51	        end
    52	
    53	        # Check if the message came from an LLM
    54	        #
    55	        # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM

    57	          assistant?
    58	        end

    61	        #

    63	        def assistant?
    64	          role == "assistant"
    65	        end
    66	
    67	        # Check if the message are system instructions
    68	        #

    72	        end

    74	        # Check if the message is a tool call

    77	        def tool?
    78	          role == "tool"
    79	        end

     3	module Langchain::LLM::Response
     4	  class ReplicateResponse < BaseResponse
     5	    def completions
     6	      # Response comes back as an array of strings, e.g.: ["Hi", "how ", "are ", "you?"]
     7	      # The first array element is missing a space at the end, so we add it manually
     8	      raw_response.output[0] += " "
     9	      [raw_response.output.join]
    10	    end

    14	    end

    18	    end
    19	
    20	    def embedding
    21	      embeddings.first
    22	    end
    23	
    24	    def embeddings
    25	      [raw_response.output]
    26	    end
    27	  end
    28	end

FILE lib/langchain/llm/ai21.rb
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Langchain::LLM
     4	  #
     5	  # Wrapper around AI21 Studio APIs.
     6	  #
     7	  # Gem requirements:
     8	  #   gem "ai21", "~> 0.2.1"
     9	  #
    10	  # Usage:
    11	  #     llm = Langchain::LLM::AI21.new(api_key: ENV["AI21_API_KEY"])
    12	  #
    13	  # @deprecated Use another LLM provider.
    14	  class AI21 < Base
    15	    DEFAULTS = {
    16	      temperature: 0.0,
    17	      model: "j2-ultra"
    18	    }.freeze
    19	
    20	    def initialize(api_key:, default_options: {})
    21	      Langchain.logger.warn "DEPRECATED: `Langchain::LLM::AI21` is deprecated, and will be removed in the next major version. Please use another LLM provider."
    22	
    23	      depends_on "ai21"
    24	
    25	      @client = ::AI21::Client.new(api_key)
    26	      @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
    27	    end
    28	
    29	    #
    30	    # Generate a completion for a given prompt
    31	    #
    32	    # @param prompt [String] The prompt to generate a completion for
    33	    # @param params [Hash] The parameters to pass to the API
    34	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::AI21Response] The completion
    35	    #
    36	    def complete(prompt:, **params)
    37	      parameters = complete_parameters params
    38	
    39	      response = client.complete(prompt, parameters)
    40	      Langchain::LLM::Response::AI21Response.new response, model: parameters[:model]
    41	    end
    42	
    43	    #
    44	    # Generate a summary for a given text
    45	    #
    46	    # @param text [String] The text to generate a summary for
    47	    # @param params [Hash] The parameters to pass to the API
    48	    # @return [String] The summary
    49	    #
    50	    def summarize(text:, **params)
    51	      response = client.summarize(text, "TEXT", params)
    52	      response.dig(:summary)
    53	      # Should we update this to also return a Langchain::LLM::AI21Response?
    54	    end
    55	
    56	    private
    57	
    58	    def complete_parameters(params)
    59	      @defaults.dup.merge(params)
    60	    end
    61	  end
    62	end

FILE lib/langchain/llm/anthropic.rb
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Langchain::LLM
     4	  #
     5	  # Wrapper around Anthropic APIs.
     6	  #
     7	  # Gem requirements:
     8	  #   gem "anthropic", "~> 1.10.0"
     9	  #
    10	  # Usage:
    11	  #     llm = Langchain::LLM::Anthropic.new(api_key: ENV["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"])
    12	  #
    13	  class Anthropic < Base
    14	    DEFAULTS = {
    15	      temperature: 0.0,
    16	      completion_model: "claude-2.1",
    17	      chat_model: "claude-sonnet-4-6",
    18	      max_tokens: 256
    19	    }.freeze
    20	
    21	    # Initialize an Anthropic LLM instance
    22	    #
    23	    # @param api_key [String] The API key to use
    24	    # @param llm_options [Hash] Options to pass to the Anthropic client
    25	    # @param default_options [Hash] Default options to use on every call to LLM, e.g.: { temperature:, completion_model:, chat_model:, max_tokens:, thinking: }
    26	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Anthropic] Langchain::LLM::Anthropic instance
    27	    def initialize(api_key:, llm_options: {}, default_options: {})
    28	      depends_on "anthropic"
    29	
    30	      @client = ::Anthropic::Client.new(api_key: api_key, **llm_options)
    31	      @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
    32	      chat_parameters.update(
    33	        model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
    34	        temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
    35	        max_tokens: {default: @defaults[:max_tokens]},
    36	        metadata: {},
    37	        system: {},
    38	        thinking: {default: @defaults[:thinking]},
    39	        request_options: {}
    40	      )
    41	      chat_parameters.ignore(:n, :user)
    42	      chat_parameters.remap(stop: :stop_sequences)
    43	    end
    44	
    45	    # Generate a completion for a given prompt
    46	    #
    47	    # @param prompt [String] Prompt to generate a completion for
    48	    # @param model [String] The model to use
    49	    # @param max_tokens_to_sample [Integer] The maximum number of tokens to sample
    50	    # @param stop_sequences [Array<String>] The stop sequences to use
    51	    # @param temperature [Float] The temperature to use
    52	    # @param top_p [Float] The top p value to use
    53	    # @param top_k [Integer] The top k value to use
    54	    # @param metadata [Hash] The metadata to use
    55	    # @param stream [Boolean] Whether to stream the response
    56	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse] The completion
    57	    def complete(
    58	      prompt:,
    59	      model: @defaults[:completion_model],
    60	      max_tokens: @defaults[:max_tokens],
    61	      stop_sequences: nil,
    62	      temperature: @defaults[:temperature],
    63	      top_p: nil,
    64	      top_k: nil,
    65	      metadata: nil,
    66	      stream: nil
    67	    )
    68	      raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if model.empty?
    69	      raise ArgumentError.new("max_tokens argument is required") if max_tokens.nil?
    70	
    71	      parameters = {
    72	        model: model,
    73	        prompt: prompt,
    74	        max_tokens_to_sample: max_tokens,
    75	        temperature: temperature
    76	      }
    77	      parameters[:stop_sequences] = stop_sequences if stop_sequences
    78	      parameters[:top_p] = top_p if top_p
    79	      parameters[:top_k] = top_k if top_k
    80	      parameters[:metadata] = metadata if metadata
    81	      parameters[:stream] = stream if stream
    82	
    83	      response = with_api_error_handling do
    84	        client.complete(parameters: parameters)
    85	      end
    86	
    87	      Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse.new(response)
    88	    end
    89	
    90	    # Generate a chat completion for given messages
    91	    #
    92	    # @param [Hash] params unified chat parmeters from [Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat::SCHEMA]
    93	    # @option params [Array<String>] :messages Input messages
    94	    # @option params [String] :model The model that will complete your prompt
    95	    # @option params [Integer] :max_tokens Maximum number of tokens to generate before stopping
    96	    # @option params [Hash] :metadata Object describing metadata about the request
    97	    # @option params [Array<String>] :stop_sequences Custom text sequences that will cause the model to stop generating
    98	    # @option params [Boolean] :stream Whether to incrementally stream the response using server-sent events
    99	    # @option params [String] :system System prompt
   100	    # @option params [Float] :temperature Amount of randomness injected into the response
   101	    # @option params [Array<String>] :tools Definitions of tools that the model may use
   102	    # @option params [Hash] :thinking Enable extended thinking mode, e.g. { type: "enabled", budget_tokens: 4000 }
   103	    # @option params [Integer] :top_k Only sample from the top K options for each subsequent token
   104	    # @option params [Float] :top_p Use nucleus sampling.
   105	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse] The chat completion
   106	    def chat(params = {}, &block)
   107	      parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)
   108	
   109	      raise ArgumentError.new("messages argument is required") if Array(parameters[:messages]).empty?
   110	      raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if parameters[:model].empty?
   111	      raise ArgumentError.new("max_tokens argument is required") if parameters[:max_tokens].nil?
   112	
   113	      if block
   114	        @response_chunks = []
   115	        parameters[:stream] = proc do |chunk|
   116	          @response_chunks << chunk
   117	          yield chunk
   118	        end
   119	      end
   120	
   121	      response = client.messages.create(parameters)
   122	
   123	      response = response_from_chunks if block
   124	      reset_response_chunks
   125	
   126	      Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse.new(response)
   127	    end
   128	
   129	    def with_api_error_handling
   130	      response = yield
   131	      return if response.empty?
   132	
   133	      raise Langchain::LLM::ApiError.new "Anthropic API error: #{response.dig("error", "message")}" if response&.dig("error")
   134	
   135	      response
   136	    end
   137	
   138	    def response_from_chunks
   139	      grouped_chunks = @response_chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["index"] }.except(nil)
   140	
   141	      usage_chunk = @response_chunks.find { |chunk| chunk["type"] == "message_delta" }
   142	      usage = usage_chunk&.dig("usage")&.transform_keys(&:to_sym)
   143	      stop_reason = usage_chunk&.dig("delta", "stop_reason")
   144	
   145	      content = grouped_chunks.map do |_index, chunks|
   146	        text = chunks.map { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "text") }.join
   147	        if !text.nil? && !text.empty?
   148	          {type: "text", text: text}
   149	        else
   150	          tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(chunks)
   151	        end
   152	      end.flatten
   153	
   154	      first_chunk = @response_chunks.first
   155	      {
   156	        id: first_chunk&.dig("id") || first_chunk&.dig("message", "id"),
   157	        model: first_chunk&.dig("model") || first_chunk&.dig("message", "model"),
   158	        content: content,
   159	        usage: usage,
   160	        role: "assistant",
   161	        stop_reason: stop_reason
   162	      }
   163	    end
   164	
   165	    def tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(chunks)
   166	      return unless (first_block = chunks.find { |chunk| chunk.dig("content_block", "type") == "tool_use" })
   167	
   168	      chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["index"] }.map do |index, chunks|
   169	        input = chunks.select { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "partial_json") }
   170	          .map! { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "partial_json") }.join
   171	        {
   172	          id: first_block.dig("content_block", "id"),
   173	          type: "tool_use",
   174	          name: first_block.dig("content_block", "name"),
   175	          input: input.empty? ? nil : JSON.parse(input).transform_keys(&:to_sym)
   176	        }
   177	      end.compact
   178	    end
   179	
   180	    private
   181	
   182	    def reset_response_chunks
   183	      @response_chunks = []
   184	    end
   185	  end
   186	end

FILE lib/langchain/llm/aws_bedrock.rb
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Langchain::LLM
     4	  # LLM interface for Aws Bedrock APIs: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/
     5	  #
     6	  # Gem requirements:
     7	  #    gem 'aws-sdk-bedrockruntime', '~> 1.1'
     8	  #
     9	  # Usage:
    10	  #    llm = Langchain::LLM::AwsBedrock.new(default_options: {})
    11	  #
    12	  class AwsBedrock < Base

   112	    # Generate a chat completion for a given prompt
   113	    # Currently only configured to work with the Anthropic provider and
   114	    # the claude-3 model family

   117	    # @option params [Array<String>] :messages The messages to generate a completion for
   118	    # @option params [String] :system The system prompt to provide instructions
   119	    # @option params [String] :model The model to use for completion defaults to @defaults[:chat_model]
   120	    # @option params [Integer] :max_tokens The maximum number of tokens to generate defaults to @defaults[:max_tokens_to_sample]
   121	    # @option params [Array<String>] :stop The stop sequences to use for completion
   122	    # @option params [Array<String>] :stop_sequences The stop sequences to use for completion
   123	    # @option params [Float] :temperature The temperature to use for completion

   128	    def chat(params = {}, &block)

   130	      parameters = compose_parameters(parameters, parameters[:model])
   131	
   132	      unless SUPPORTED_CHAT_COMPLETION_PROVIDERS.include?(provider_name(parameters[:model]))
   133	        raise "Chat provider #{parameters[:model]} is not supported."
   134	      end
   135	
   136	      if block
   137	        response_chunks = []
   138	
   139	        client.invoke_model_with_response_stream(
   140	          model_id: parameters[:model],
   141	          body: parameters.except(:model).to_json,
   142	          content_type: "application/json",
   143	          accept: "application/json"
   144	        ) do |stream|
   145	          stream.on_event do |event|
   146	            chunk = JSON.parse(event.bytes)
   147	            response_chunks << chunk
   148	
   149	            yield chunk
   150	          end
   151	        end
   152	
   153	        response_from_chunks(response_chunks)
   154	      else
   155	        response = client.invoke_model({
   156	          model_id: parameters[:model],
   157	          body: parameters.except(:model).to_json,
   158	          content_type: "application/json",
   159	          accept: "application/json"
   160	        })
   161	
   162	        parse_response(response, parameters[:model])
   163	      end
   164	    end

   166	    private

   168	    def parse_model_id(model_id)
   169	      model_id

   171	        .split(".")
   172	    end
   173	
   174	    def provider_name(model_id)
   175	      parse_model_id(model_id).first.to_sym
   176	    end
   177	
   178	    def model_name(model_id)
   179	      parse_model_id(model_id).last
   180	    end
   181	
   182	    def completion_provider
   183	      @defaults[:completion_model].split(".").first.to_sym
   184	    end
   185	
   186	    def embedding_provider
   187	      @defaults[:embedding_model].split(".").first.to_sym
   188	    end
   189	
   190	    def wrap_prompt(prompt)
   191	      if completion_provider == :anthropic
   192	        "\n\nHuman: #{prompt}\n\nAssistant:"
   193	      else
   194	        prompt
   195	      end
   196	    end
   197	
   198	    def max_tokens_key
   199	      if completion_provider == :anthropic
   200	        :max_tokens_to_sample
   201	      elsif completion_provider == :cohere
   202	        :max_tokens
   203	      elsif completion_provider == :ai21
   204	        :maxTokens
   205	      end
   206	    end
   207	
   208	    def compose_parameters(params, model_id)
   209	      if provider_name(model_id) == :anthropic
   210	        compose_parameters_anthropic(params)
   211	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :cohere
   212	        compose_parameters_cohere(params)
   213	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :ai21
   214	        params
   215	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :meta
   216	        params
   217	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :mistral
   218	        params
   219	      end
   220	    end
   221	
   222	    def compose_embedding_parameters(params)
   223	      if embedding_provider == :amazon
   224	        compose_embedding_parameters_amazon params
   225	      elsif embedding_provider == :cohere
   226	        compose_embedding_parameters_cohere params
   227	      end
   228	    end
   229	
   230	    def parse_response(response, model_id)
   231	      if provider_name(model_id) == :anthropic
   232	        Langchain::LLM::Response::AwsBedrockAnthropicResponse.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string))
   233	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :cohere
   234	        Langchain::LLM::Response::CohereResponse.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string))
   235	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :ai21
   236	        Langchain::LLM::Response::AI21Response.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string, symbolize_names: true))
   237	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :meta
   238	        Langchain::LLM::Response::AwsBedrockMetaResponse.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string))
   239	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :mistral
   240	        Langchain::LLM::Response::MistralAIResponse.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string))

    14	  # - {Langchain::LLM::GoogleVertexAI}
    15	  # - {Langchain::LLM::HuggingFace}
    16	  # - {Langchain::LLM::OpenAI}
    17	  # - {Langchain::LLM::Replicate}
    18	  #
    19	  # @abstract
    20	  class Base

    82	    # Generate a chat completion for given messages

    90	    # @option params [String] :system System prompt

    92	    # @option params [Array<String>] :tools Definitions of tools that the model may use
    93	    # @option params [Integer] :top_k Only sample from the top K options for each subsequent token
    94	    # @option params [Float] :top_p Use nucleus sampling.
    95	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::CohereResponse] The chat completion
    96	    def chat(params = {})

   110	    # Generate a summary in English for a given text

     3	module Langchain::LLM
     4	  #
     5	  # Wrapper around the Google Vertex AI APIs: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai
     6	  #
     7	  # Gem requirements:
     8	  #     gem "googleauth"
     9	  #
    10	  # Usage:
    11	  #     llm = Langchain::LLM::GoogleVertexAI.new(project_id: ENV["GOOGLE_VERTEX_AI_PROJECT_ID"], region: "us-central1")
    12	  #
    13	  class GoogleVertexAI < Base
    14	    DEFAULTS = {
    15	      temperature: 0.1,
    16	      max_output_tokens: 1000,
    17	      top_p: 0.8,
    18	      top_k: 40,
    19	      dimensions: 768,
    20	      embedding_model: "textembedding-gecko",
    21	      chat_model: "gemini-1.0-pro"
    22	    }.freeze
    23	
    24	    # Google Cloud has a project id and a specific region of deployment.
    25	    # For GenAI-related things, a safe choice is us-central1.
    26	    attr_reader :defaults, :url, :authorizer
    27	
    28	    def initialize(project_id:, region:, default_options: {})
    29	      depends_on "googleauth"
    30	
    31	      @authorizer = ::Google::Auth.get_application_default([
    32	        "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform",
    33	        "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/generative-language.retriever"
    34	      ])
    35	      proj_id = project_id || @authorizer.project_id || @authorizer.quota_project_id
    36	      @url = "https://#{region}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/#{proj_id}/locations/#{region}/publishers/google/models/"
    37	
    38	      @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
    39	
    40	      chat_parameters.update(
    41	        model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
    42	        temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
    43	        safety_settings: {default: @defaults[:safety_settings]}
    44	      )
    45	      chat_parameters.remap(
    46	        messages: :contents,
    47	        system: :system_instruction,
    48	        tool_choice: :tool_config
    49	      )
    50	    end
    51	
    52	    #
    53	    # Generate an embedding for a given text
    54	    #
    55	    # @param text [String] The text to generate an embedding for
    56	    # @param model [String] ID of the model to use
    57	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::GoogleGeminiResponse] Response object
    58	    #
    59	    def embed(
    60	      text:,
    61	      model: @defaults[:embedding_model]
    62	    )
    63	      params = {instances: [{content: text}]}
    64	
    65	      uri = URI("#{url}#{model}:predict")
    66	
    67	      parsed_response = http_post(uri, params)
    68	
    69	      Langchain::LLM::Response::GoogleGeminiResponse.new(parsed_response, model: model)
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    # Generate a chat completion for given messages
    73	    #
    74	    # @param messages [Array<Hash>] Input messages
    75	    # @param model [String] The model that will complete your prompt
    76	    # @param tools [Array<Hash>] The tools to use
    77	    # @param tool_choice [String] The tool choice to use
    78	    # @param system [String] The system instruction to use
    79	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::GoogleGeminiResponse] Response object
    80	    def chat(params = {})
    81	      params[:system] = {parts: [{text: params[:system]}]} if params[:system]
    82	      params[:tools] = {function_declarations: params[:tools]} if params[:tools]
    83	
    84	      raise ArgumentError.new("messages argument is required") if Array(params[:messages]).empty?
    85	
    86	      parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)
    87	      parameters[:generation_config] = {temperature: parameters.delete(:temperature)} if parameters[:temperature]
    88	
    89	      uri = URI("#{url}#{parameters[:model]}:generateContent")
    90	
    91	      parsed_response = http_post(uri, parameters)
    92	
    93	      wrapped_response = Langchain::LLM::Response::GoogleGeminiResponse.new(parsed_response, model: parameters[:model])
    94	
    95	      if wrapped_response.chat_completion || Array(wrapped_response.tool_calls).any?
    96	        wrapped_response
    97	      else
    98	        raise StandardError.new(parsed_response)
    99	      end
   100	    end
   101	
   102	    private
   103	
   104	    def http_post(url, params)
   105	      http = Net::HTTP.new(url.hostname, url.port)
   106	      http.use_ssl = url.scheme == "https"
   107	      http.set_debug_output(Langchain.logger) if Langchain.logger.debug?
   108	
   109	      request = Net::HTTP::Post.new(url)
   110	      request.content_type = "application/json"
   111	      request["Authorization"] = "Bearer #{@authorizer.fetch_access_token!["access_token"]}"
   112	      request.body = params.to_json
   113	
   114	      response = http.request(request)
   115	
   116	      JSON.parse(response.body)
   117	    end
   118	  end
   119	end

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Langchain::LLM
     4	  # A wrapper around the LlamaCpp.rb library
     5	  #
     6	  # Gem requirements:
     7	  #     gem "llama_cpp"
     8	  #
     9	  # Usage:
    10	  #     llama = Langchain::LLM::LlamaCpp.new(
    11	  #       model_path: ENV["LLAMACPP_MODEL_PATH"],
    12	  #       n_gpu_layers: Integer(ENV["LLAMACPP_N_GPU_LAYERS"]),
    13	  #       n_threads: Integer(ENV["LLAMACPP_N_THREADS"])
    14	  #     )
    15	  #
    16	  # @deprecated Use {Langchain::LLM::Ollama} for self-hosted LLM inference.
    17	  class LlamaCpp < Base
    18	    attr_accessor :model_path, :n_gpu_layers, :n_ctx, :seed
    19	    attr_writer :n_threads
    20	
    21	    # @param model_path [String] The path to the model to use
    22	    # @param n_gpu_layers [Integer] The number of GPU layers to use
    23	    # @param n_ctx [Integer] The number of context tokens to use
    24	    # @param n_threads [Integer] The CPU number of threads to use
    25	    # @param seed [Integer] The seed to use
    26	    def initialize(model_path:, n_gpu_layers: 1, n_ctx: 2048, n_threads: 1, seed: 0)
    27	      Langchain.logger.warn "DEPRECATED: `Langchain::LLM::LlamaCpp` is deprecated, and will be removed in the next major version. Please use `Langchain::LLM::Ollama` for self-hosted LLM inference."
    28	
    29	      depends_on "llama_cpp"
    30	
    31	      @model_path = model_path
    32	      @n_gpu_layers = n_gpu_layers
    33	      @n_ctx = n_ctx
    34	      @n_threads = n_threads
    35	      @seed = seed
    36	    end
    37	
    38	    # @param text [String] The text to embed
    39	    # @return [Array<Float>] The embedding
    40	    def embed(text:)
    41	      # contexts are kinda stateful when it comes to embeddings, so allocate one each time
    42	      context = embedding_context
    43	
    44	      embedding_input = @model.tokenize(text: text, add_bos: true)
    45	      return unless embedding_input.size.positive?
    46	
    47	      context.eval(tokens: embedding_input, n_past: 0)
    48	      Langchain::LLM::Response::LlamaCppResponse.new(context, model: context.model.desc)
    49	    end
    50	
    51	    # @param prompt [String] The prompt to complete
    52	    # @param n_predict [Integer] The number of tokens to predict
    53	    # @return [String] The completed prompt
    54	    def complete(prompt:, n_predict: 128)
    55	      # contexts do not appear to be stateful when it comes to completion, so re-use the same one
    56	      context = completion_context
    57	      ::LLaMACpp.generate(context, prompt, n_predict: n_predict)
    58	    end
    59	
    60	    private
    61	
    62	    def n_threads
    63	      # Use the maximum number of CPU threads available, if not configured
    64	      @n_threads ||= `sysctl -n hw.ncpu`.strip.to_i
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    def build_context_params(embeddings: false)
    68	      context_params = ::LLaMACpp::ContextParams.new
    69	
    70	      context_params.seed = seed
    71	      context_params.n_ctx = n_ctx
    72	      context_params.n_threads = n_threads
    73	      context_params.embedding = embeddings
    74	
    75	      context_params
    76	    end
    77	
    78	    def build_model_params
    79	      model_params = ::LLaMACpp::ModelParams.new
    80	      model_params.n_gpu_layers = n_gpu_layers
    81	
    82	      model_params
    83	    end
    84	
    85	    def build_model(embeddings: false)
    86	      return @model if defined?(@model)
    87	      @model = ::LLaMACpp::Model.new(model_path: model_path, params: build_model_params)
    88	    end
    89	
    90	    def build_completion_context
    91	      ::LLaMACpp::Context.new(model: build_model, params: build_context_params(embeddings: false))
    92	    end
    93	
    94	    def build_embedding_context
    95	      ::LLaMACpp::Context.new(model: build_model, params: build_context_params(embeddings: true))
    96	    end
    97	
    98	    def completion_context
    99	      @completion_context ||= build_completion_context
   100	    end
   101	
   102	    def embedding_context
   103	      @embedding_context ||= build_embedding_context
   104	    end
   105	  end
   106	end

    30	    # Initialize an OpenAI LLM instance

    43	      @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)

    56	    # Generate an embedding for a given text

    62	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::OpenAIResponse] Response object
    63	    def embed(

    67	      user: nil,
    68	      dimensions: @defaults[:dimensions]

    70	      raise ArgumentError.new("text argument is required") if text.empty?
    71	      raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if model.empty?

    77	      }

    79	      parameters[:user] = user if user

    97	    # rubocop:disable Style/ArgumentsForwarding
    98	    # Generate a completion for a given prompt
    99	    #
   100	    # @param prompt [String] The prompt to generate a completion for
   101	    # @param params [Hash] The parameters to pass to the `chat()` method
   102	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::OpenAIResponse] Response object
   103	    # @deprecated Use {chat} instead.
   104	    def complete(prompt:, **params)
   105	      Langchain.logger.warn "DEPRECATED: `Langchain::LLM::OpenAI#complete` is deprecated, and will be removed in the next major version. Use `Langchain::LLM::OpenAI#chat` instead."
   106	
   107	      if params[:stop_sequences]
   108	        params[:stop] = params.delete(:stop_sequences)
   109	      end
   110	      # Should we still accept the `messages: []` parameter here?
   111	      messages = [{role: "user", content: prompt}]
   112	      chat(messages: messages, **params)
   113	    end
   114	
   115	    # rubocop:enable Style/ArgumentsForwarding
   116	
   117	    # Generate a chat completion for given messages.
   118	    #
   119	    # @param [Hash] params unified chat parmeters from [Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat::SCHEMA]
   120	    # @option params [Array<Hash>] :messages List of messages comprising the conversation so far
   121	    # @option params [String] :model ID of the model to use
   122	    def chat(params = {}, &block)
   123	      parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)
   124	
   125	      raise ArgumentError.new("messages argument is required") if Array(parameters[:messages]).empty?
   126	      raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if parameters[:model].to_s.empty?
   127	      if parameters[:tool_choice] && Array(parameters[:tools]).empty?
   128	        raise ArgumentError.new("'tool_choice' is only allowed when 'tools' are specified.")
   129	      end
   130	
   131	      if block
   132	        @response_chunks = []
   133	        parameters[:stream_options] = {include_usage: true}
   134	        parameters[:stream] = proc do |chunk, _bytesize|
   135	          chunk_content = chunk.dig("choices", 0) || {}
   136	          @response_chunks << chunk
   137	          yield chunk_content
   138	        end
   139	      end
   140	
   141	      response = with_api_error_handling do
   142	        client.chat(parameters: parameters)
   143	      end
   144	
   145	      response = response_from_chunks if block
   146	      reset_response_chunks
   147	
   148	      Langchain::LLM::Response::OpenAIResponse.new(response)
   149	    end
   150	
   151	    # Generate a summary for a given text
   152	    #
   153	    # @param text [String] The text to generate a summary for
   154	    # @return [String] The summary
   155	    def summarize(text:)
   156	      prompt_template = Langchain::Prompt.load_from_path(
   157	        file_path: Langchain.root.join("langchain/llm/prompts/summarize_template.yaml")
   158	      )
   159	      prompt = prompt_template.format(text: text)
   160	
   161	      complete(prompt: prompt)
   162	    end
   163	
   164	    def default_dimensions
   165	      @defaults[:dimensions] || EMBEDDING_SIZES.fetch(defaults[:embedding_model])
   166	    end
   167	
   168	    private
   169	
   170	    attr_reader :response_chunks
   171	
   172	    def reset_response_chunks
   173	      @response_chunks = []
   174	    end
   175	
   176	    def with_api_error_handling
   177	      response = yield
   178	      return if response.nil? || response.empty?
   179	
   180	      raise Langchain::LLM::ApiError.new "OpenAI API error: #{response.dig("error", "message")}" if response&.dig("error")
   181	
   182	      response
   183	    end
   184	
   185	    def response_from_chunks
   186	      grouped_chunks = @response_chunks
   187	        .group_by { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "index") }
   188	        .except(nil) # the last chunk (that contains the token usage) has no index
   189	      final_choices = grouped_chunks.map do |index, chunks|
   190	        {
   191	          "index" => index,
   192	          "message" => {
   193	            "role" => "assistant",
   194	            "content" => chunks.map { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "content") }.join,
   195	            "tool_calls" => tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(chunks)
   196	          }.compact,
   197	          "finish_reason" => chunks.last.dig("choices", 0, "finish_reason")
   198	        }
   199	      end
   200	      @response_chunks.first&.slice("id", "object", "created", "model")&.merge({"choices" => final_choices, "usage" => @response_chunks.last["usage"]})
   201	    end
   202	
   203	    def tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(choice_chunks)
   204	      tool_call_chunks = choice_chunks.select { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls") }
   205	      return nil if tool_call_chunks.empty?
   206	
   207	      tool_call_chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "index") }.map do |index, chunks|
   208	        first_chunk = chunks.first
   209	
   210	        {
   211	          "id" => first_chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "id"),
   212	          "type" => first_chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "type"),
   213	          "function" => {
   214	            "name" => first_chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "function", "name"),
   215	            "arguments" => chunks.map { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "function", "arguments") }.join
   216	          }
   217	        }
   218	      end
   219	    end
   220	  end
   221	end

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Langchain::LLM
     4	  class UnifiedParameters
     5	    include Enumerable
     6	
     7	    attr_reader :schema, :aliases, :parameters, :ignored, :remapped
     8	
     9	    class Null < self
    10	      def initialize(parameters: {})
    11	        super(schema: {}, parameters: parameters)
    12	      end
    13	    end
    14	
    15	    def initialize(schema:, parameters: {})
    16	      @schema = schema || {}
    17	      @aliases = {}
    18	      @remapped = {}
    19	      @ignored = Set.new
    20	      @schema.each do |name, param|
    21	        @aliases[name] = Set.new(Array(param[:aliases])) if param[:aliases]
    22	      end
    23	      @parameters = to_params(parameters.to_h) if !parameters.to_h.empty?
    24	    end
    25	
    26	    def to_params(params = {})
    27	      # if params are provided, reset any previously initialized
    28	      @parameters = params if !params.empty?
    29	      @parameters = (@parameters || {}).merge!(params).slice(*schema.keys)
    30	      @aliases.each do |field, aliased_keys|
    31	        # favor existing keys in case of conflicts,
    32	        # and check for multiples
    33	        aliased_keys.each do |alias_key|
    34	          @parameters[field] ||= params[alias_key] if value_present?(params[alias_key])
    35	        end
    36	      end
    37	      @schema.each do |field, param_options|
    38	        param_options ||= {}
    39	        default = param_options[:default]
    40	        @parameters[field] ||= default if value_present?(default)
    41	      end
    42	      @remapped.each do |field, renamed_field|
    43	        @parameters[renamed_field] = @parameters[field] if value_present?(@parameters[field])
    44	      end
    45	      @parameters = @parameters.except(*@ignored + @remapped.keys)
    46	    end
    47	
    48	    def remap(field_map)
    49	      @remapped ||= {}
    50	      @remapped.merge!(field_map)
    51	      field_map.each do |field, renamed_field|
    52	        @schema[renamed_field] = @schema[field]
    53	      end
    54	    end
    55	
    56	    def update(schema = {})
    57	      @schema.merge!(schema)
    58	      schema.each do |name, param|
    59	        if param[:aliases]
    60	          @aliases[name] ||= Set.new
    61	          @aliases[name] << param[:aliases]
    62	        end
    63	      end
    64	      self
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    def ignore(*field_names)
    68	      @ignored.merge(field_names)
    69	    end
    70	
    71	    def alias_field(field_name, as:)
    72	      @aliases[field_name] ||= Set.new
    73	      @aliases[field_name] << as
    74	    end
    75	
    76	    def to_h
    77	      @parameters.to_h
    78	    end
    79	
    80	    def each(&block)
    81	      to_params.each(&block)
    82	    end
    83	
    84	    def <=>(other)
    85	      to_params.<=>(other.to_params)
    86	    end
    87	
    88	    def [](key)
    89	      to_params[key]
    90	    end
    91	
    92	    private
    93	
    94	    def value_present?(value)
    95	      !value.nil? && (!value.is_a?(Enumerable) || !value.empty?)
    96	    end
    97	  end
    98	end
