1	require "logger"
     2	require "langchain/version"
     3	require "langchain/engine"
     4	
     5	module Langchain
     6	  class << self
     7	    # @return [Logger]
     8	    attr_accessor :logger
     9	    # @return [Pathname]
    10	    attr_reader :root
    11	  end
    12	
    13	  module Errors
    14	    class BaseError < StandardError; end
    15	  end
    16	
    17	  module Colorizer
    18	    class << self
    19	      def red(str)
    20	        "\e[31m#{str}\e[0m"
    21	      end
    22	
    23	      def green(str)
    24	        "\e[32m#{str}\e[0m"
    25	      end
    26	
    27	      def yellow(str)
    28	        "\e[33m#{str}\e[0m"
    29	      end
    30	
    31	      def blue(str)
    32	        "\e[34m#{str}\e[0m"
    33	      end
    34	
    35	      def colorize_logger_msg(msg, severity)
    36	        return msg unless msg.is_a?(String)
    37	
    38	        return red(msg) if severity.to_sym == :ERROR
    39	        return yellow(msg) if severity.to_sym == :WARN
    40	        msg
    41	      end
    42	    end
    43	  end
    44	
    45	  LOGGER_OPTIONS = {
    46	    progname: "Langchain.rb",
    47	
    48	    formatter: ->(severity, time, progname, msg) do
    49	      Logger::Formatter.new.call(
    50	        severity,
    51	        time,
    52	        "[#{progname}]",
    53	        Colorizer.colorize_logger_msg(msg, severity)
    54	      )
    55	    end
    56	  }.freeze
    57	
    58	  self.logger ||= ::Logger.new($stdout, **LOGGER_OPTIONS)
    59	  @root = Pathname.new(__dir__)
    60	end

37	module Langchain::ToolDefinition
    38	  # Defines a function for the tool
    39	  #
    40	  # @param method_name [Symbol] Name of the method to define
    41	  # @param description [String] Description of the function
    42	  # @yield Block that defines the parameters for the function
    43	  def define_function(method_name, description:, &block)
    44	    function_schemas.add_function(method_name:, description:, &block)
    45	  end
    46	
    47	  # Returns the FunctionSchemas instance for this tool
    48	  #
    49	  # @return [FunctionSchemas] The FunctionSchemas instance
    50	  def function_schemas
    51	    @function_schemas ||= FunctionSchemas.new(tool_name)
    52	  end
    53	
    54	  # Returns the snake_case version of the class name as the tool's name
    55	  #
    56	  # @return [String] The snake_case version of the class name
    57	  def tool_name
    58	    @tool_name ||= name
    59	      .gsub("::", "_")
    60	      .gsub(/(?<=[A-Z])(?=[A-Z][a-z])|(?<=[a-z\d])(?=[A-Z])/, "_")
    61	      .downcase
    62	  end
    63	
    64	  def self.extended(base)
    65	    base.include(InstanceMethods)
    66	  end
    67	
    68	  module InstanceMethods
    69	    # Create a tool response
    70	    # @param content [String, nil] The content of the tool response
    71	    # @param image_url [String, nil] The URL of an image
    72	    # @return [Langchain::ToolResponse] The tool response
    73	    def tool_response(content: nil, image_url: nil)
    74	      Langchain::ToolResponse.new(content: content, image_url: image_url)
    75	    end
    76	  end
    77	
    78	  # Manages schemas for functions
    79	  class FunctionSchemas
    80	    def initialize(tool_name)
    81	      @schemas = {}
    82	      @tool_name = tool_name
    83	    end
    84	
    85	    # Adds a function to the schemas
    86	    #
    87	    # @param method_name [Symbol] Name of the method to add
    88	    # @param description [String] Description of the function
    89	    # @yield Block that defines the parameters for the function
    90	    # @raise [ArgumentError] If a block is defined and no parameters are specified for the function
    91	    def add_function(method_name:, description:, &block)
    92	      name = "#{@tool_name}__#{method_name}"
    93	
    94	      if block_given? # rubocop:disable Performance/BlockGivenWithExplicitBlock
    95	        parameters = ParameterBuilder.new(parent_type: "object").build(&block)
    96	
    97	        if parameters[:properties].empty?
    98	          raise ArgumentError, "Function parameters must have at least one property defined within it, if a block is provided"
    99	        end
   100	      end
   101	
   102	      @schemas[method_name] = {
   103	        type: "function",
   104	        function: {name:, description:, parameters:}.compact
   105	      }
   106	    end
   107	
   108	    # Converts schemas to OpenAI-compatible format
   109	    #
   110	    # @return [String] JSON string of schemas in OpenAI format
   111	    def to_openai_format
   112	      @schemas.values
   113	    end
   114	
   115	    # Converts schemas to Anthropic-compatible format
   116	    #
   117	    # @return [String] JSON string of schemas in Anthropic format
   118	    def to_anthropic_format
   119	      @schemas.values.map do |schema|
   120	        # Adds a default input_schema if no parameters are present
   121	        schema[:function][:parameters] ||= {
   122	          type: "object",
   123	          properties: {},
   124	          required: []
   125	        }
   126	
   127	        schema[:function].transform_keys(parameters: :input_schema)
   128	      end
   129	    end
   130	
   131	    # Converts schemas to Google Gemini-compatible format
   132	    #
   133	    # @return [String] JSON string of schemas in Google Gemini format
   134	    def to_google_gemini_format
   135	      @schemas.values.map { |schema| schema[:function] }
   136	    end
   137	  end
   138	
   139	  # Builds parameter schemas for functions
   140	  class ParameterBuilder
   141	    VALID_TYPES = %w[object array string number integer boolean].freeze
   142	
   143	    def initialize(parent_type:)
   144	      @schema = (parent_type == "object") ? {type: "object", properties: {}, required: []} : {}
   145	      @parent_type = parent_type
   146	    end
   147	
   148	    # Builds the parameter schema
   149	    #
   150	    # @yield Block that defines the properties of the schema
   151	    # @return [Hash] The built schema
   152	    def build(&block)
   153	      instance_eval(&block)
   154	      @schema
   155	    end
   156	
   157	    # Defines a property in the schema
   158	    #
   159	    # @param name [Symbol] Name of the property (required only for a parent of type object)
   160	    # @param type [String] Type of the property

   112	    # Generate a chat completion for a given prompt
   113	    # Currently only configured to work with the Anthropic provider and
   114	    # the claude-3 model family
   115	    #
   116	    # @param [Hash] params unified chat parmeters from [Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat::SCHEMA]
   117	    # @option params [Array<String>] :messages The messages to generate a completion for
   118	    # @option params [String] :system The system prompt to provide instructions
   119	    # @option params [String] :model The model to use for completion defaults to @defaults[:chat_model]
   120	    # @option params [Integer] :max_tokens The maximum number of tokens to generate defaults to @defaults[:max_tokens_to_sample]
   121	    # @option params [Array<String>] :stop The stop sequences to use for completion
   122	    # @option params [Array<String>] :stop_sequences The stop sequences to use for completion
   123	    # @option params [Float] :temperature The temperature to use for completion
   124	    # @option params [Float] :top_p Use nucleus sampling.
   125	    # @option params [Integer] :top_k Only sample from the top K options for each subsequent token
   126	    # @yield [Hash] Provides chunks of the response as they are received
   127	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse] Response object
   128	    def chat(params = {}, &block)
   129	      parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)
   130	      parameters = compose_parameters(parameters, parameters[:model])
   131	
   132	      unless SUPPORTED_CHAT_COMPLETION_PROVIDERS.include?(provider_name(parameters[:model]))
   133	        raise "Chat provider #{parameters[:model]} is not supported."
   134	      end
   135	
   136	      if block
   137	        response_chunks = []
   138	
   139	        client.invoke_model_with_response_stream(
   140	          model_id: parameters[:model],
   141	          body: parameters.except(:model).to_json,
   142	          content_type: "application/json",
   143	          accept: "application/json"
   144	        ) do |stream|
   145	          stream.on_event do |event|
   146	            chunk = JSON.parse(event.bytes)
   147	            response_chunks << chunk
   148	
   149	            yield chunk
   150	          end
   151	        end
   152	
   153	        response_from_chunks(response_chunks)
   154	      else
   155	        response = client.invoke_model({
   156	          model_id: parameters[:model],
   157	          body: parameters.except(:model).to_json,
   158	          content_type: "application/json",
   159	          accept: "application/json"
   160	        })
   161	
   162	        parse_response(response, parameters[:model])
   163	      end
   164	    end

   208	    def compose_parameters(params, model_id)
   209	      if provider_name(model_id) == :anthropic

   211	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :cohere
   212	        compose_parameters_cohere(params)
   213	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :ai21
   214	        params
   215	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :meta
   216	        params
   217	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :mistral

   221	
   222	    def compose_embedding_parameters(params)
   223	      if embedding_provider == :amazon
   224	        compose_embedding_parameters_amazon params
   225	      elsif embedding_provider == :cohere
   226	        compose_embedding_parameters_cohere params
   227	      end

   230	    def parse_response(response, model_id)
   231	      if provider_name(model_id) == :anthropic
   232	        Langchain::LLM::Response::AwsBedrockAnthropicResponse.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string))
   233	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :cohere
   234	        Langchain::LLM::Response::CohereResponse.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string))
   235	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :ai21
   236	        Langchain::LLM::Response::AI21Response.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string, symbolize_names: true))
   237	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :meta
   238	        Langchain::LLM::Response::AwsBedrockMetaResponse.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string))
   239	      elsif provider_name(model_id) == :mistral
   240	        Langchain::LLM::Response::MistralAIResponse.new(JSON.parse(response.body.string))
   241	      end
   242	    end
   243	
   244	    def parse_embedding_response(response)
   245	      json_response = JSON.parse(response.body.string)
   246	
   247	      if embedding_provider == :amazon
   248	        Langchain::LLM::Response::AwsTitanResponse.new(json_response)
   249	      elsif embedding_provider == :cohere
   250	        Langchain::LLM::Response::CohereResponse.new(json_response)
   251	      end
   252	    end

   254	    def compose_embedding_parameters_amazon(params)
   255	      default_params = @defaults.merge(params)
   256	
   257	      {
   258	        inputText: default_params[:text],
   259	        dimensions: default_params[:dimensions],
   260	        normalize: default_params[:normalize]
   261	      }.compact
   262	    end
   263	
   264	    def compose_embedding_parameters_cohere(params)
   265	      default_params = @defaults.merge(params)
   266	
   267	      {
   268	        texts: [default_params[:text]],
   269	        truncate: default_params[:truncate],
   270	        input_type: default_params[:input_type],
   271	        embedding_types: default_params[:embedding_types]
   272	      }.compact
   273	    end
   274	
   275	    def compose_parameters_cohere(params)
   276	      default_params = @defaults.merge(params)
   277	
   278	      {
   279	        max_tokens: default_params[:max_tokens_to_sample],
   280	        temperature: default_params[:temperature],
   281	        p: default_params[:top_p],
   282	        k: default_params[:top_k],
   283	        stop_sequences: default_params[:stop_sequences]
   284	      }
   285	    end
   286	
   287	    def compose_parameters_anthropic(params)
   288	      params.merge(anthropic_version: "bedrock-2023-05-31")
   289	    end
   290	
   291	    def response_from_chunks(chunks)
   292	      raw_response = {}
   293	
   294	      chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["type"] }.each do |type, chunks|
   295	        case type
   296	        when "message_start"
   297	          raw_response = chunks.first["message"]
   298	        when "content_block_start"
   299	          raw_response["content"] = chunks.map { |chunk| chunk["content_block"] }
   300	        when "content_block_delta"
   301	          chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["index"] }.each do |index, deltas|
   302	            deltas.group_by { |delta| delta.dig("delta", "type") }.each do |type, deltas|
   303	              case type
   304	              when "text_delta"
   305	                raw_response["content"][index]["text"] = deltas.map { |delta| delta.dig("delta", "text") }.join
   306	              when "input_json_delta"
   307	                json_string = deltas.map { |delta| delta.dig("delta", "partial_json") }.join
   308	                raw_response["content"][index]["input"] = json_string.empty? ? {} : JSON.parse(json_string)
   309	              end
   310	            end
   311	          end
   312	        when "message_delta"
   313	          chunks.each do |chunk|
   314	            raw_response = raw_response.merge(chunk["delta"])
   315	            raw_response["usage"] = raw_response["usage"].merge(chunk["usage"]) if chunk["usage"]
   316	          end
   317	        end
   318	      end
   319	
   320	      Langchain::LLM::Response::AwsBedrockAnthropicResponse.new(raw_response)
   321	    end
   322	  end
   323	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Langchain::LLM
     4	  # Usage:
     5	  #     llm = Langchain::LLM::GoogleGemini.new(api_key: ENV['GOOGLE_GEMINI_API_KEY'])
     6	  class GoogleGemini < Base
     7	    DEFAULTS = {
     8	      chat_model: "gemini-1.5-pro-latest",
     9	      embedding_model: "text-embedding-004",
    10	      temperature: 0.0
    11	    }
    12	
    13	    attr_reader :defaults, :api_key
    14	
    15	    def initialize(api_key:, default_options: {})
    16	      @api_key = api_key
    17	      @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
    18	
    19	      chat_parameters.update(
    20	        model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
    21	        temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
    22	        generation_config: {default: nil},
    23	        safety_settings: {default: @defaults[:safety_settings]}
    24	      )
    25	      chat_parameters.remap(
    26	        messages: :contents,
    27	        system: :system_instruction,
    28	        tool_choice: :tool_config
    29	      )
    30	    end
    31	
    32	    # Generate a chat completion for a given prompt
    33	    #
    34	    # @param messages [Array<Hash>] List of messages comprising the conversation so far
    35	    # @param model [String] The model to use
    36	    # @param tools [Array<Hash>] A list of Tools the model may use to generate the next response
    37	    # @param tool_choice [String] Specifies the mode in which function calling should execute. If unspecified, the default value will be set to AUTO. Possible values: AUTO, ANY, NONE
    38	    # @param system [String] Developer set system instruction
    39	    def chat(params = {})
    40	      params[:system] = {parts: [{text: params[:system]}]} if params[:system]
    41	      params[:tools] = {function_declarations: params[:tools]} if params[:tools]
    42	
    43	      raise ArgumentError.new("messages argument is required") if Array(params[:messages]).empty?
    44	
    45	      parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)
    46	      parameters[:generation_config] ||= {}
    47	      parameters[:generation_config][:temperature] ||= parameters[:temperature] if parameters[:temperature]
    48	      parameters.delete(:temperature)
    49	      parameters[:generation_config][:top_p] ||= parameters[:top_p] if parameters[:top_p]
    50	      parameters.delete(:top_p)
    51	      parameters[:generation_config][:top_k] ||= parameters[:top_k] if parameters[:top_k]
    52	      parameters.delete(:top_k)
    53	      parameters[:generation_config][:max_output_tokens] ||= parameters[:max_tokens] if parameters[:max_tokens]
    54	      parameters.delete(:max_tokens)
    55	      parameters[:generation_config][:response_mime_type] ||= parameters[:response_format] if parameters[:response_format]
    56	      parameters.delete(:response_format)
    57	      parameters[:generation_config][:stop_sequences] ||= parameters[:stop] if parameters[:stop]
    58	      parameters.delete(:stop)
    59	
    60	      uri = URI("https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/#{parameters[:model]}:generateContent?key=#{api_key}")
    61	
    62	      parsed_response = http_post(uri, parameters)
    63	
    64	      wrapped_response = Langchain::LLM::Response::GoogleGeminiResponse.new(parsed_response, model: parameters[:model])
    65	
    66	      if wrapped_response.chat_completion || Array(wrapped_response.tool_calls).any?
    67	        wrapped_response
    68	      else
    69	        raise StandardError.new(parsed_response)
    70	      end
    71	    end
    72	
    73	    def embed(
    74	      text:,
    75	      model: @defaults[:embedding_model]
    76	    )
    77	      params = {
    78	        content: {
    79	          parts: [
    80	            {
    81	              text: text
    82	            }
    83	          ]
    84	        }
    85	      }
    86	
    87	      uri = URI("https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/#{model}:embedContent?key=#{api_key}")
    88	
    89	      parsed_response = http_post(uri, params)
    90	
    91	      Langchain::LLM::Response::GoogleGeminiResponse.new(parsed_response, model: model)
    92	    end
    93	
    94	    private
    95	
    96	    def http_post(url, params)
    97	      http = Net::HTTP.new(url.hostname, url.port)
    98	      http.use_ssl = url.scheme == "https"
    99	      http.set_debug_output(Langchain.logger) if Langchain.logger.debug?
   100	
   101	      request = Net::HTTP::Post.new(url)
   102	      request.content_type = "application/json"
   103	      request.body = params.to_json
   104	
   105	      response = http.request(request)
   106	
   107	      JSON.parse(response.body)
   108	    end
   109	  end
   110	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Langchain::LLM
     4	  #
     5	  # Wrapper around the Google Vertex AI APIs: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai
     6	  #
     7	  # Gem requirements:
     8	  #     gem "googleauth"
     9	  #
    10	  # Usage:
    11	  #     llm = Langchain::LLM::GoogleVertexAI.new(project_id: ENV["GOOGLE_VERTEX_AI_PROJECT_ID"], region: "us-central1")
    12	  #
    13	  class GoogleVertexAI < Base
    14	    DEFAULTS = {
    15	      temperature: 0.1,
    16	      max_output_tokens: 1000,
    17	      top_p: 0.8,
    18	      top_k: 40,
    19	      dimensions: 768,
    20	      embedding_model: "textembedding-gecko",
    21	      chat_model: "gemini-1.0-pro"
    22	    }.freeze
    23	
    24	    # Google Cloud has a project id and a specific region of deployment.
    25	    # For GenAI-related things, a safe choice is us-central1.
    26	    attr_reader :defaults, :url, :authorizer
    27	
    28	    def initialize(project_id:, region:, default_options: {})
    29	      depends_on "googleauth"
    30	
    31	      @authorizer = ::Google::Auth.get_application_default([
    32	        "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform",
    33	        "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/generative-language.retriever"
    34	      ])
    35	      proj_id = project_id || @authorizer.project_id || @authorizer.quota_project_id
    36	      @url = "https://#{region}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/#{proj_id}/locations/#{region}/publishers/google/models/"
    37	
    38	      @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
    39	
    40	      chat_parameters.update(
    41	        model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
    42	        temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
    43	        safety_settings: {default: @defaults[:safety_settings]}
    44	      )
    45	      chat_parameters.remap(
    46	        messages: :contents,
    47	        system: :system_instruction,
    48	        tool_choice: :tool_config
    49	      )
    50	    end
    51	
    52	    #
    53	    # Generate an embedding for a given text
    54	    #
    55	    # @param text [String] The text to generate an embedding for
    56	    # @param model [String] ID of the model to use
    57	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::GoogleGeminiResponse] Response object
    58	    #
    59	    def embed(
    60	      text:,
    61	      model: @defaults[:embedding_model]
    62	    )
    63	      params = {instances: [{content: text}]}
    64	
    65	      uri = URI("#{url}#{model}:predict")
    66	
    67	      parsed_response = http_post(uri, params)
    68	
    69	      Langchain::LLM::Response::GoogleGeminiResponse.new(parsed_response, model: model)
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    # Generate a chat completion for given messages
    73	    #
    74	    # @param messages [Array<Hash>] Input messages
    75	    # @param model [String] The model that will complete your prompt
    76	    # @param tools [Array<Hash>] The tools to use
    77	    # @param tool_choice [String] The tool choice to use
    78	    # @param system [String] The system instruction to use
    79	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::GoogleGeminiResponse] Response object
    80	    def chat(params = {})
    81	      params[:system] = {parts: [{text: params[:system]}]} if params[:system]
    82	      params[:tools] = {function_declarations: params[:tools]} if params[:tools]
    83	
    84	      raise ArgumentError.new("messages argument is required") if Array(params[:messages]).empty?
    85	
    86	      parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)
    87	      parameters[:generation_config] = {temperature: parameters.delete(:temperature)} if parameters[:temperature]
    88	
    89	      uri = URI("#{url}#{parameters[:model]}:generateContent")
    90	
    91	      parsed_response = http_post(uri, parameters)
    92	
    93	      wrapped_response = Langchain::LLM::Response::GoogleGeminiResponse.new(parsed_response, model: parameters[:model])
    94	
    95	      if wrapped_response.chat_completion || Array(wrapped_response.tool_calls).any?
    96	        wrapped_response
    97	      else
    98	        raise StandardError.new(parsed_response)
    99	      end
   100	    end
   101	
   102	    private
   103	
   104	    def http_post(url, params)
   105	      http = Net::HTTP.new(url.hostname, url.port)
   106	      http.use_ssl = url.scheme == "https"
   107	      http.set_debug_output(Langchain.logger) if Langchain.logger.debug?
   108	
   109	      request = Net::HTTP::Post.new(url)
   110	      request.content_type = "application/json"
   111	      request["Authorization"] = "Bearer #{@authorizer.fetch_access_token!["access_token"]}"
   112	      request.body = params.to_json
   113	
   114	      response = http.request(request)
   115	
   116	      JSON.parse(response.body)
   117	    end
   118	  end
   119	end

     3	module Langchain::LLM
     4	  #
     5	  # Wrapper around the Cohere API.
     6	  #
     7	  # Gem requirements:
     8	  #     gem "cohere-ruby", "~> 0.9.6"
     9	  #
    10	  # Usage:
    11	  #     llm = Langchain::LLM::Cohere.new(api_key: ENV["COHERE_API_KEY"])
    12	  #
    13	  class Cohere < Base
    14	    DEFAULTS = {
    15	      temperature: 0.0,
    16	      completion_model: "command",
    17	      chat_model: "command-r-plus",
    18	      embedding_model: "small",
    19	      dimensions: 1024,
    20	      truncate: "START"
    21	    }.freeze
    22	
    23	    def initialize(api_key:, default_options: {})
    24	      depends_on "cohere-ruby", req: "cohere"
    25	
    26	      @client = ::Cohere::Client.new(api_key: api_key)
    27	      @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
    28	      chat_parameters.update(
    29	        model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
    30	        temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
    31	        response_format: {default: @defaults[:response_format]}
    32	      )
    33	      chat_parameters.remap(
    34	        system: :preamble,
    35	        messages: :chat_history,
    36	        stop: :stop_sequences,
    37	        top_k: :k,
    38	        top_p: :p
    39	      )
    40	    end
    41	
    42	    #
    43	    # Generate an embedding for a given text
    44	    #
    45	    # @param text [String] The text to generate an embedding for
    46	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::CohereResponse] Response object
    47	    #
    48	    def embed(text:)
    49	      response = client.embed(
    50	        texts: [text],
    51	        model: @defaults[:embedding_model]
    52	      )
    53	
    54	      Langchain::LLM::Response::CohereResponse.new response, model: @defaults[:embedding_model]
    55	    end
    56	
    57	    #
    58	    # Generate a completion for a given prompt
    59	    #
    60	    # @param prompt [String] The prompt to generate a completion for
    61	    # @param params[:stop_sequences]
    62	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::CohereResponse] Response object
    63	    #
    64	    def complete(prompt:, **params)
    65	      default_params = {
    66	        prompt: prompt,
    67	        temperature: @defaults[:temperature],
    68	        model: @defaults[:completion_model],
    69	        truncate: @defaults[:truncate]
    70	      }
    71	
    72	      if params[:stop_sequences]
    73	        default_params[:stop_sequences] = params.delete(:stop_sequences)
    74	      end
    75	
    76	      default_params.merge!(params)
    77	
    78	      response = client.generate(**default_params)
    79	      Langchain::LLM::Response::CohereResponse.new response, model: @defaults[:completion_model]
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    # Generate a chat completion for given messages
    83	    #
    84	    # @param [Hash] params unified chat parmeters from [Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat::SCHEMA]
    85	    # @option params [Array<String>] :messages Input messages
    86	    # @option params [String] :model The model that will complete your prompt
    87	    # @option params [Integer] :max_tokens Maximum number of tokens to generate before stopping
    88	    # @option params [Array<String>] :stop Custom text sequences that will cause the model to stop generating
    89	    # @option params [Boolean] :stream Whether to incrementally stream the response using server-sent events
    90	    # @option params [String] :system System prompt
    91	    # @option params [Float] :temperature Amount of randomness injected into the response
    92	    # @option params [Array<String>] :tools Definitions of tools that the model may use
    93	    # @option params [Integer] :top_k Only sample from the top K options for each subsequent token
    94	    # @option params [Float] :top_p Use nucleus sampling.
    95	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::CohereResponse] The chat completion
    96	    def chat(params = {})
    97	      raise ArgumentError.new("messages argument is required") if Array(params[:messages]).empty?
    98	
    99	      parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)
   100	
   101	      # Cohere API requires `message:` parameter to be sent separately from `chat_history:`.
   102	      # We extract the last message from the messages param.
   103	      parameters[:message] = parameters[:chat_history].pop&.dig(:message)
   104	
   105	      response = client.chat(**parameters)
   106	
   107	      Langchain::LLM::Response::CohereResponse.new(response)
   108	    end
   109	
   110	    # Generate a summary in English for a given text
   111	    #
   112	    # More parameters available to extend this method with: https://github.com/andreibondarev/cohere-ruby/blob/0.9.4/lib/cohere/client.rb#L107-L115
   113	    #
   114	    # @param text [String] The text to generate a summary for
   115	    # @return [String] The summary
   116	    def summarize(text:)
   117	      response = client.summarize(text: text)
   118	      response.dig("summary")
   119	    end
   120	  end
   121	end

     3	module Langchain::LLM
     4	  # LLM interface for Azure OpenAI Service APIs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/
     5	  #
     6	  # Gem requirements:
     7	  #    gem "ruby-openai", "~> 6.3.0"
     8	  #
     9	  # Usage:
    10	  #    llm = Langchain::LLM::Azure.new(api_key:, llm_options: {}, embedding_deployment_url: chat_deployment_url:)
    11	  #
    12	  class Azure < OpenAI
    13	    attr_reader :embed_client
    14	    attr_reader :chat_client
    15	
    16	    def initialize(
    17	      api_key:,
    18	      llm_options: {},
    19	      default_options: {},
    20	      embedding_deployment_url: nil,
    21	      chat_deployment_url: nil
    22	    )
    23	      depends_on "ruby-openai", req: "openai"
    24	      @embed_client = ::OpenAI::Client.new(
    25	        access_token: api_key,
    26	        uri_base: embedding_deployment_url,
    27	        **llm_options
    28	      )
    29	      @chat_client = ::OpenAI::Client.new(
    30	        access_token: api_key,
    31	        uri_base: chat_deployment_url,
    32	        **llm_options
    33	      )
    34	      @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
    35	      chat_parameters.update(
    36	        model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
    37	        logprobs: {},
    38	        top_logprobs: {},
    39	        n: {default: @defaults[:n]},
    40	        temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
    41	        user: {},
    42	        response_format: {default: @defaults[:response_format]}
    43	      )
    44	      chat_parameters.ignore(:top_k)
    45	    end
    46	
    47	    def embed(...)
    48	      @client = @embed_client
    49	      super
    50	    end
    51	
    52	    def complete(...)
    53	      @client = @chat_client
    54	      super
    55	    end
    56	
    57	    def chat(...)
    58	      @client = @chat_client
    59	      super
    60	    end
    61	  end
    62	end

     3	module Langchain::LLM
     4	  # Interface to Ollama API.
     5	  # Available models: https://ollama.ai/library
     6	  #
     7	  # Gem requirements:
     8	  #    gem "faraday"
     9	  #
    10	  # Usage:
    11	  #    llm = Langchain::LLM::Ollama.new(url: ENV["OLLAMA_URL"], default_options: {})
    12	  #
    13	  class Ollama < Base
    14	    attr_reader :url, :defaults
    15	
    16	    DEFAULTS = {
    17	      temperature: 0.0,
    18	      completion_model: "llama3.2",
    19	      embedding_model: "llama3.2",
    20	      chat_model: "llama3.2",
    21	      options: {}
    22	    }.freeze
    23	
    24	    EMBEDDING_SIZES = {
    25	      codellama: 4_096,
    26	      "dolphin-mixtral": 4_096,
    27	      llama2: 4_096,
    28	      llama3: 4_096,
    29	      "llama3.1": 4_096,
    30	      "llama3.2": 3_072,
    31	      llava: 4_096,
    32	      mistral: 4_096,
    33	      "mistral-openorca": 4_096,
    34	      mixtral: 4_096,
    35	      tinydolphin: 2_048
    36	    }.freeze
    37	
    38	    # Initialize the Ollama client
    39	    # @param url [String] The URL of the Ollama instance
    40	    # @param api_key [String] The API key to use. This is optional and used when you expose Ollama API using Open WebUI
    41	    # @param default_options [Hash] The default options to use
    42	    #
    43	    def initialize(url: "http://localhost:11434", api_key: nil, default_options: {})
    44	      depends_on "faraday"
    45	      @url = url
    46	      @api_key = api_key
    47	      @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
    48	      chat_parameters.update(
    49	        model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
    50	        temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
    51	        template: {},
    52	        stream: {default: false},
    53	        response_format: {default: @defaults[:response_format]},
    54	        options: {default: @defaults[:options]}
    55	      )
    56	      chat_parameters.remap(response_format: :format)
    57	    end
    58	
    59	    # Returns the # of vector dimensions for the embeddings
    60	    # @return [Integer] The # of vector dimensions
    61	    def default_dimensions
    62	      # since Ollama can run multiple models, look it up or generate an embedding and return the size
    63	      @default_dimensions ||=
    64	        EMBEDDING_SIZES.fetch(defaults[:embedding_model].to_sym) do
    65	          embed(text: "test").embedding.size
    66	        end
    67	    end
    68	
    69	    #
    70	    # Generate the completion for a given prompt
    71	    #
    72	    # @param prompt [String] The prompt to complete
    73	    # @param model [String] The model to use
    74	    #   For a list of valid parameters and values, see:
    75	    #   https://github.com/jmorganca/ollama/blob/main/docs/modelfile.md#valid-parameters-and-values
    76	    # @option block [Proc] Receive the intermediate responses as a stream of +OllamaResponse+ objects.
    77	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::OllamaResponse] Response object
    78	    #
    79	    # Example:
    80	    #
    81	    #  final_resp = ollama.complete(prompt:) { |resp| print resp.completion }
    82	    #  final_resp.total_tokens
    83	    #
    84	    def complete(
    85	      prompt:,
    86	      model: defaults[:completion_model],
    87	      images: nil,
    88	      format: nil,
    89	      system: nil,
    90	      template: nil,
    91	      context: nil,
    92	      raw: nil,
    93	      mirostat: nil,
    94	      mirostat_eta: nil,
    95	      mirostat_tau: nil,
    96	      num_ctx: nil,
    97	      num_gqa: nil,
    98	      num_gpu: nil,
    99	      num_thread: nil,
   100	      repeat_last_n: nil,
   101	      repeat_penalty: nil,
   102	      temperature: defaults[:temperature],
   103	      seed: nil,
   104	      stop: nil,
   105	      tfs_z: nil,
   106	      num_predict: nil,
   107	      top_k: nil,
   108	      top_p: nil,
   109	      stop_sequences: nil,
   110	      &block
   111	    )
   112	      if stop_sequences
   113	        stop = stop_sequences
   114	      end
   115	
   116	      parameters = {
   117	        prompt: prompt,
   118	        model: model,
   119	        images: images,
   120	        format: format,
   121	        system: system,
   122	        template: template,
   123	        context: context,
   124	        stream: block_given?, # rubocop:disable Performance/BlockGivenWithExplicitBlock
   125	        raw: raw
   126	      }.compact
   127	
   128	      llm_parameters = {
   129	        mirostat: mirostat,
   130	        mirostat_eta: mirostat_eta,
   131	        mirostat_tau: mirostat_tau,
   132	        num_ctx: num_ctx,
   133	        num_gqa: num_gqa,
   134	        num_gpu: num_gpu,
   135	        num_thread: num_thread,
   136	        repeat_last_n: repeat_last_n,
   137	        repeat_penalty: repeat_penalty,
   138	        temperature: temperature,
   139	        seed: seed,
   140	        stop: stop,
   141	        tfs_z: tfs_z,
   142	        num_predict: num_predict,
   143	        top_k: top_k,
   144	        top_p: top_p
   145	      }
   146	
   147	      parameters[:options] = llm_parameters.compact
   148	      responses_stream = []
   149	
   150	      client.post("api/generate", parameters) do |req|
   151	        req.options.on_data = json_responses_chunk_handler do |parsed_chunk|
   152	          responses_stream << parsed_chunk
   153	
   154	          block&.call(Langchain::LLM::Response::OllamaResponse.new(parsed_chunk, model: parameters[:model]))
   155	        end
   156	      end
   157	
   158	      generate_final_completion_response(responses_stream, parameters[:model])
   159	    end
   160	
   161	    # Generate a chat completion
   162	    #
   163	    # @param messages [Array] The chat messages
   164	    # @param model [String] The model to use
   165	    # @param params [Hash] Unified chat parmeters from [Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat::SCHEMA]
   166	    # @option params [Array<Hash>] :messages Array of messages
   167	    # @option params [String] :model Model name
   168	    # @option params [String] :format Format to return a response in. Currently the only accepted value is `json`
   169	    # @option params [Float] :temperature The temperature to use
   170	    # @option params [String] :template The prompt template to use (overrides what is defined in the `Modelfile`)
   171	    # @option block [Proc] Receive the intermediate responses as a stream of +OllamaResponse+ objects.
   172	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::OllamaResponse] Response object
   173	    #
   174	    # Example:
   175	    #
   176	    #  final_resp = ollama.chat(messages:) { |resp| print resp.chat_completion }
   177	    #  final_resp.total_tokens
   178	    #
   179	    # The message object has the following fields:
   180	    #   role: the role of the message, either system, user or assistant
   181	    #   content: the content of the message
   182	    #   images (optional): a list of images to include in the message (for multimodal models such as llava)
   183	    def chat(messages:, model: nil, **params, &block)
   184	      parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params.merge(messages:, model:, stream: block_given?)) # rubocop:disable Performance/BlockGivenWithExplicitBlock
   185	      responses_stream = []
   186	
   187	      client.post("api/chat", parameters) do |req|
   188	        req.options.on_data = json_responses_chunk_handler do |parsed_chunk|
   189	          responses_stream << parsed_chunk
   190	
   191	          block&.call(Langchain::LLM::Response::OllamaResponse.new(parsed_chunk, model: parameters[:model]))
   192	        end
   193	      end
   194	
   195	      generate_final_chat_completion_response(responses_stream, parameters[:model])
   196	    end
   197	
   198	    #
   199	    # Generate an embedding for a given text
   200	    #
   201	    # @param text [String] The text to generate an embedding for
   202	    # @param model [String] The model to use
   203	    # @param options [Hash] The options to use
   204	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::OllamaResponse] Response object
   205	    #
   206	    def embed(
   207	      text:,
   208	      model: defaults[:embedding_model],
   209	      mirostat: nil,
   210	      mirostat_eta: nil,
   211	      mirostat_tau: nil,
   212	      num_ctx: nil,
   213	      num_gqa: nil,
   214	      num_gpu: nil,
   215	      num_thread: nil,
   216	      repeat_last_n: nil,
   217	      repeat_penalty: nil,
   218	      temperature: defaults[:temperature],
   219	      seed: nil,
   220	      stop: nil,
   221	      tfs_z: nil,
   222	      num_predict: nil,
   223	      top_k: nil,
   224	      top_p: nil
   225	    )
   226	      parameters = {
   227	        model: model,
   228	        input: Array(text)
   229	      }.compact
   230	
   231	      llm_parameters = {
   232	        mirostat: mirostat,
   233	        mirostat_eta: mirostat_eta,
   234	        mirostat_tau: mirostat_tau,
   235	        num_ctx: num_ctx,
   236	        num_gqa: num_gqa,
   237	        num_gpu: num_gpu,
   238	        num_thread: num_thread,
   239	        repeat_last_n: repeat_last_n,
   240	        repeat_penalty: repeat_penalty,
   241	        temperature: temperature,
   242	        seed: seed,
   243	        stop: stop,
   244	        tfs_z: tfs_z,
   245	        num_predict: num_predict,
   246	        top_k: top_k,
   247	        top_p: top_p
   248	      }
   249	
   250	      parameters[:options] = llm_parameters.compact
   251	
   252	      response = client.post("api/embed") do |req|
   253	        req.body = parameters
   254	      end
   255	
   256	      Langchain::LLM::Response::OllamaResponse.new(response.body, model: parameters[:model])
   257	    end
   258	
   259	    # Generate a summary for a given text
   260	    #
   261	    # @param text [String] The text to generate a summary for
   262	    # @return [String] The summary
   263	    def summarize(text:)
   264	      prompt_template = Langchain::Prompt.load_from_path(
   265	        file_path: Langchain.root.join("langchain/llm/prompts/ollama/summarize_template.yaml")
   266	      )
   267	      prompt = prompt_template.format(text: text)
   268	
   269	      complete(prompt: prompt)
   270	    end
   271	
   272	    private
   273	
   274	    def client
   275	      @client ||= Faraday.new(url: url, headers: auth_headers) do |conn|
   276	        conn.request :json
   277	        conn.response :json
   278	        conn.response :raise_error
   279	        conn.response :logger, Langchain.logger, {headers: true, bodies: true, errors: true}
   280	      end
   281	    end
   282	
   283	    def auth_headers
   284	      return unless @api_key
   285	
   286	      {"Authorization" => "Bearer #{@api_key}"}
   287	    end
   288	
   289	    def json_responses_chunk_handler(&block)
   290	      proc do |chunk, _size|
   291	        chunk.split("\n").each do |chunk_line|
   292	          parsed_chunk = JSON.parse(chunk_line)
   293	          block.call(parsed_chunk)
   294	        end
   295	      end
   296	    end
   297	
   298	    def generate_final_completion_response(responses_stream, model)
   299	      final_response = responses_stream.last.merge(
   300	        "response" => responses_stream.map { |resp| resp["response"] }.join
   301	      )
   302	
   303	      Langchain::LLM::Response::OllamaResponse.new(final_response, model: model)
   304	    end
   305	
   306	    # BUG: If streamed, this method does not currently return the tool_calls response.
   307	    def generate_final_chat_completion_response(responses_stream, model)
   308	      final_response = responses_stream.last
   309	      final_response["message"]["content"] = responses_stream.map { |resp| resp.dig("message", "content") }.join
   310	
   311	      Langchain::LLM::Response::OllamaResponse.new(final_response, model: model)
   312	    end
   313	  end
   314	end
